description:
This package provides a gui program as well as a rviz plugin to publish static transforms.
  Both support the transformation between various Euler angle representations.
  The rviz plugin also allows to configure the transform with an interactive marker.
readme: ROS package tools facilitating definition (static) ROS transforms. extensions rviz: EulerProperty class allowing definition arbitrary sets Euler angles conversion RotationProperty class combining EulerProperty QuaternionProperty provide flexible means entering orientation information. interactive transform publisher rviz::Display plugin allowing define transforms rviz interactive marker. interactively perform frame transformations. cmdline utilities: static_transform_publisher adapted version tf2_ros’s source code, providing fine-grained command-line options define orientations arbitrary set Euler angles. static_transform_publisher_gui interactive version static_transform_publisher allowing modify transform interactively.

description:
This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in

description:
This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

    heartbeat times out.

C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

Python implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

description:
Provides a toolchain running through the robot calibration process. This
     involves capturing calibration data, estimating parameters, and
     then updating the URDF.

Runs an optimization to estimate the a robot's kinematic parameters. This package is a
    generic rewrite of pr2_calibration_estimation.

This package contains a collection of launch files that can be helpful in configuring
    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

This package defines messages for storing calibration samples
     to be used in full robot calibration procedures. This package
     is still unstable. Expect the messages to change.

    which is based on Michael Ferguson's calibration code

    This package is still experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Tools for calculating the intersection of interval messages coming
    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Provides a node that reports how long a subset of joints has been

     Expect its APIs to change.

     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

Listens on a ImageFeatures topic, and waits for the data to settle.
     This package is experimental and unstable.

description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package
The cob_omni_drive_controller package
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

description: The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...
This packagae implements the special homing procedure that is needed for old cob4/raw bases

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

readme: ROS OpenCV camera driver easy capture video device cv::VideoCapture OpenCV. searching ROS2 driver, check cv_camera_node node camera_info_manager dealing camera_info. calibration data set, dummy values width height. Publish ~image_raw (sensor_msgs/Image) ~camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) Service ~set_camera_info (sensor_msgs/SetCameraInfo) Parameters ~rate (double, default: publish rate ~device_id (int, default: capture device ~device_path (string, default: path camera device file, /dev/video0. ~frame_id (string, default: “camera”) frame_id message header. ~image_width (int) set capture image width. ~image_height (int) set capture image height. ~camera_info_url (string) url camera info yaml. ~file (string, default: movie file device. ~capture_delay (double, default: estimated duration capturing receiving image. ~rescale_camera_info (bool, default: false) rescale camera calibration info automatically. ~camera_name (bool, default: frame_id) camera camera_info_manager. Supports CV_CAP_PROP_*, params. ~cv_cap_prop_pos_msec (double) ~cv_cap_prop_pos_avi_ratio (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_width (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_height (double) ~cv_cap_prop_fps (double) ~cv_cap_prop_fourcc (double) ~cv_cap_prop_frame_count (double) ~cv_cap_prop_format (double) ~cv_cap_prop_mode (double) ~cv_cap_prop_brightness (double) ~cv_cap_prop_contrast (double) ~cv_cap_prop_saturation (double) ~cv_cap_prop_hue (double) ~cv_cap_prop_gain (double) ~cv_cap_prop_exposure (double) ~cv_cap_prop_convert_rgb (double) ~cv_cap_prop_rectification (double) ~cv_cap_prop_iso_speed (double) supports props. Hernan Badino! ~property_$(i)_code (int) set code property ~property_$(i)_value, start ~property_$(i)_value (double) set ~property_$(i)_code set property code rosrun cv_camera cv_camera_node _property_0_code:=404 _property_0_value:=1 set ~property_1_code ~property_1_value. description: CAN interface to the Universal Lat/Lon Controller (ULC) firmware
Package to translate ROS messages to and from CAN messages to interact with the Universal Lat/Lon Controller (ULC) firmware
readme: dataspeed_ulc_can ulc_node node ROS users command Universal Lat/Lon Controller (ULC) ROS messages manually construct populate messages. node subscribes command topics alter behavior node. recommended command topic time. Subscribed Topics ulc_cmd (dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcCmd) Input command ULC. addition speed steering command inputs, topic configures behavior ULC. user turn speed steering components ULC switch shifting steering modes, configure longitudinal lateral acceleration limits. cmd_vel (geometry_msgs/Twist) Simplified interface ULC. messages topic command ULC: speed component ULC activated tracks linear.x field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units steering component ULC activated yaw rate mode tracks angular.z field geometry_msgs/Twist message, assuming units rad/s longitudinal lateral acceleration limits default settings outlined ULC User’s Guide cmd_vel_stamped (geometry_msgs/TwistStamped) header incoming geometry_msgs/TwistStamped messages treated identically cmd_vel topic. can_rx (can_msgs/Frame) topic listens traffic Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus data publish messages ulc_report topic. Published Topics can_tx (can_msgs/Frame) topic transmits ULC command configuration messages Dataspeed ADAS Kit bus control ULC running ADAS Kit firmware. messages constructed based user publishing command messages input topics. ulc_report(dataspeed_ulc_msgs/UlcReport) Feedback data ULC running ADAS Kit firmware.

The ddynamic_reconfigure_python package contains
    a class to instantiate dynamic reconfigure servers on the fly
    registering variables
readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed. 

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).
     
     They will come as the need arises.

A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to 
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.
Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.
This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.
Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

Any tools relating to mathematical geometry. 
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing 
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these 
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it 
  to be done consistently across platforms.
Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots, 
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include 
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier 
     and are multithread-safe.
Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded 
     programming tools. These are usually different on different 
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support
	
	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.
Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.
Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.
Metapackage bringing all of ecl together.

Includes basic manipulation related structures as well as a few 
    algorithms currently used at Yujin Robot.

Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just 
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers
Makes it possible to run the ros_ethercat_loop without using sudo. Forked from pr2-grant
readme: ethercat_grant repo pr2-grant utility. run ros_ethercat_loop requiring root privileges. Installation installed package apt-get you’re good recommended compiled package source, you’ll copy resulting ethercat_grant executable /usr/local/bin add sticky bit sudo chmod /usr/local/bin/ethercat_grant. ethercat_grant sudo anymore running ethercat main loop. launch-prefix="ethercat_grant" launch files ros_ethercat_loop. releasing package (developers change scripts/postinst post installation script, manually copied, commited pushed branch debian/ROS_DISTRO/ethercat_grant debian directory ROS_DISTRO hydro, indigo…) https://github.com/shadow-robot/ethercat_grant-release/tree/debian/indigo/ethercat_grant/debian http://answers.ros.org/question/191779/add-postinstall-rule-for-deb-package-creation/

description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with
This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description: Messages used by Fawkes to interact with ROS.
readme: Fawkes ROS Message Types repository ROS message, service, action specifications access Fawkes features ROS NavGraph navgraph topological graph path planning storing location-specific semantic information related ground map. message types relevant. NavGraph message: navgraph specification- NavGraphEdge sub-message: Edge representation navgraph. NavGraphNode sub-message: Node representation navgraph. NavGraphProperty sub-message: Key-value pair represent properties nodes edges. NavGraphGetPairwiseCosts service Calculates costs pairs nodes graph. Note costly operation. provide full cost specification, planning problems. NavGraphPathCost sub-message: Denote costs path nodes. [NavGraphSearchPath service](srv/NavGraphSearchPath.srv]: Search path nodes navgraph. path returns path sequence nodes total cost. NavGraphGoto action: ROS action move specific node graph. messages processed navgraph-breakout plugin fawkes_navgraph node, reads monitors navgraph YAML file provide API. examples confer rcll_fawkes_sim rosplan_interface_behaviorengine packages. Position exchange object positions universal reference, generic Fawkes ROS object transfer plugin ros-position-3d. Position3D message Position space pose visibility history. history initialization, positive number consecutive positive sightings object, negative number consecutive sensor data frames object visible. history flip negative number positive number visibility toggles. node implement tolerate number frames toggling stability. Behavior Engine Integration interface Lua-based Behavior Engine (project) ROS execute basic actions. SkillStatus message: Describes current status skill execution. ExecSkill action: ROS action execute skill string. PCL Database Fawkes functionality store, retrieve, merge pointclouds MongoDB database. functionality ROS (paper video). StorePointCloud service: Store point cloud database collection. RetrievePointCloud service: Retrieve point cloud database collection time. Result topic point clouds stored (enables trigger external processing pipeline simply invoking restore point cloud). MergePointClouds service: Merge point clouds number timestamps. algorithm paper. result topic. Note requires transform information recorded, typically mongodb_log ROS mongodb-log Fawkes.

description: FCL is a collision checking library
readme: FCL Flexible Collision Library Linux Windows Coverage FCL library performing types proximity queries pair geometric models composed triangles. Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. description: A meta-package to aggregate all the FlexBE packages
flexbe_core provides the core components for the FlexBE behavior engine.
flexbe_input enables to send data to onboard behavior when required.
flexbe_mirror implements functionality to remotely mirror an executed behavior.
flexbe_msgs provides the messages used by FlexBE.
flexbe_onboard implements the robot-side of the behavior engine from where all behaviors are started.

flexbe_states provides a collection of predefined states.
     Feel free to add new states.
flexbe_testing provides a framework for unit testing states.
flexbe_widget implements some smaller scripts for the behavior engine.
flexbe_app provides a user interface (editor + runtime control) for the FlexBE behavior engine.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

description: camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.
Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens

    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.

description: ROS interface to the 3Dconnexion SpaceNavigator 6DOF joystick.
readme: Spacenav Nodes spacenav_node Published topics spacenav/offset (geometry_msgs/Vector3) Publishes linear component joystick’s position. description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn

description: The respeaker_ros package
readme: respeaker_ros ROS Package Respeaker Mic Array Supported Devices Respeaker Mic Array Preparation Install package Assumed ROS properly installed. install package apt-get. sudo apt-get install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-respeaker-ros build source. mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty.git ~/catkin_ws source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash rosdep install --from-paths src catkin config --init catkin build respeaker_ros source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Register respeaker udev rules access USB device permission user space. udev, give permission respeaker device automatically. run command followings install setting file: roscd respeaker_ros sudo $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/config/60-respeaker.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/60-respeaker.rules sudo systemctl restart udev re-connect device. Update firmware git clone https://github.com/respeaker/usb_4_mic_array.git usb_4_mic_array sudo python dfu.py --download 6_channels_firmware.bin channels version Run executables roslaunch respeaker_ros sample_respeaker.launch rostopic echo /sound_direction Result DoA rostopic echo /sound_localization Result DoA Pose rostopic echo /is_speeching Result VAD rostopic echo /audio Raw audio rostopic echo /speech_audio Audio data speeching rostopic echo /speech_to_text Voice recognition Voice recognition result Japanese rostopic echo --filter "print(m.transcript[0])" /speech_to_text rostopic echo --filter "print('transcript: [%s]\n---'%(', '.join(map(str, m.transcript))))" /speech_to_text rostopic echo /speech_to_text ascii2uni set parameters `dynamic_reconfigure`. sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rqt-reconfigure Install rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure set LED color, publish desired color: rostopic pub /status_led std_msgs/ColorRGBA cases Voice Recognition ros_speech_recognition julius_ros Notes configuration file dynamic_reconfigure package created automatically reading parameters devices. rare case, configuration file updated followings: Connect device computer. Run generator script. rosrun respeaker_ros respeaker_gencfg.py updated configuration file $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/cfg/Respeaker.cfg. The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

readme: jsk_teleop_joy package Introduction package physical teleoperatoin joy sticks, game controllers MIDI instruments. Supported game controllers package supports game controllers: XBox360 controller Controller (Bluetooth) Controller (USB) order controller Bluetooth, instruction. Architecture jsk_teleop_joy jsk_teleop_joy main script joy_main.py) based plugin architecture. ROS packages plugins jsk_teleop_joy plugin represents function pose effector”. enable plugins choose plugin “Pushing Select Button”. jsk_teleop_joy plugins box VerboseStatus JoyPose6D JoyFootstep JoyFootstepPlanner JoyFootstepPlannerDemo JoyMoveIt JoyGoPos implement plugin write xml file define plugins. Export xml file ROS package manifest.xml package.xml. implement plugin Write xml file define plugins jsk_teleop_joy reads plugin definition xml file plugin.xml. xml <library> <class name="Foo" type="your_package.foo"> </class> </library> xml file <library> tag top level. define plugins <class> tag. attribute means plugin. choose plugins launch files type attribute means python class plugin. jsk_teleop_joy instantiate plugin class thie type Export xml file ROS package export xml file <export> tag <jsk_teleop_joy> tag manifest.xml package.xml. files, manifest.xml, package.xml, good examples. Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. VerboseStatus Plugin good implement plugin. __init__ method plugins required inherits jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin call JSKJoyPlugin.__init__ __init__ constructor. class VerboseStatus(jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin): def __init__(self): jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin.__init__(self, 'VerbosePlugin') joyCB method time jsk_teleop_joy receives /joy message, calls joyCB method active plugin. def joyCB(self, status, history): rospy.loginfo('analog left (status.left_analog_x, status.left_analog_y)) argument joyCB instance JoyStatus. JoyStatus XboxStatus, PS3Status PS3WiredStatus. means latest message /joy. classes provide interface don’t care controller user hand, argument joyCB (history), sequence JoyStatus. instance StatusHistory. means hisotry JoyStatus. SELECT button jsk_teleop_joy system occupies SELECT button SELECT button plugin. SELECT button switching plugins. description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample input data FFT. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. spectrum_filter.py script filter spectrum. Publishing topics ~output (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Filtered spectrum data (low_cut_freq-high_cut_freq). Subscribing topics ~input (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Original spectrum data. Parameters ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate data generation original spectrum data. ~high_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit maximum frequency output spectrum. ~low_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit minimum frequency output spectrum. spectrum_to_spectrogram.py script convert spectrum spectrogram. Publishing topics ~spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image) Spectrogram data, concatenation spectrum time series. Image format Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~image_height (Int, default: Number vertical (frequency axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~image_width (Int, default: Number horizontal (time axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~spectrogram_period (Double, default: Period store spectrum data create spectrogram topic. ~publish_rate (Double, default: image_width spectrogram_period) Publish rate spectrogram topic. audio_amplitude_plot.py script publish audio amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Audio amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size sound input plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate audio amplitude image topic. data_amplitude_plot.py Generalized version audio_amplitude_plot.py. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Data amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data amplitude plot published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size input data plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate data amplitude image topic. spectrum_plot.py script publish frequency amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Frequency amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. readme: Sound Classification ROS package classify sound stream. Contents Setup Usage Quick demo Setup Install ROS. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

      with this package.

      catkin still. In the interim we use a unary pre-catkin stack named kobuki_description.

A controller ensuring the safe operation of Kobuki.

    The SafetyController keeps track of bumper, cliff and wheel drop events. In case of the first two,

    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (bumper pressed etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!

description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.

Meta-package of libraries for processing laser data, including converting laser data
      into 3D representations.

Dependencies: libnabo depends Eigen, modern matrix linear-algebra library. libnabo works version Eigen. libnabo optionally depends Boost, general library, Python bindings. Credit: libnabo developed Stéphane Magnenat part work ASL-ETH libnabo maintained NorLab interested pure-Rust version, check repository Supported Architecture libnabo tested build system architecture arm64/v8 Ubuntu bionic focal Note: libnabo reportedly works MacOs OsX (latest) Windows (latest) Version Release Note release libnabo introduces integration norlab-build-system (NBS) git submodule codebase development testing. Execute clone repository submodule: git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/norlab-ulaval/libnabo.git libnabo previously cloned, execute fetch submodule git submodule update --remote --recursive --init Contributing Instructions contributing_instructions.md instructions related bug reporting, code contribution setting libnabo-build-system workstation speed local development workflow. Docker images Run commands pull run libnabo docker container docker pull norlabulaval/libnabo:latest-ubuntu-focal docker run norlabulaval/libnabo:latest-ubuntu-focal libnabo image tags dockerhub. install docker related dependencies ubuntu, execute ./build_system/nabo_utility_script Execute docker tools install script docker daemon, docker compose, docker buildx bash nabo_install_docker_tools.bash Compilation conveniences, build script Unix bash libnabo_dependencies_installer.bash flag list optional flag bash libnabo_installer.bash [<optional flag>] control, follow commands: SRC_DIR=`pwd` BUILD_DIR=${SRC_DIR}/build mkdir ${BUILD_DIR} ${BUILD_DIR} cmake ${SRC_DIR} cmake --build ${BUILD_DIR} sudo cmake --build ${BUILD_DIR} --target install lines compile libnabo build sub-directory install system-wide. uninstall libnabo, simply run sudo uninstall build directory. Compilation options libnabo compilation options, CMake: SHARED_LIBS (boolean, default: false): true, build shared library, build static library Documentation access https://norlab-ulaval.github.io/libnabo/. Alternatively, generate locally typing: doc Prerequisites operating system provide Eigen, Boost build Python bindings. Eigen downloaded extracted. Add libnabo CMake project find_package(libnabo REQUIRED) target_link_libraries(example PUBLIC ${libnabo_LIBRARIES}) target_include_directories(example PUBLIC ${libnabo_INCLUDE_DIRS}) Usage libnabo easy assuming working floats point set query point find nearest neighbours #include "nabo/nabo.h" namespace Nabo; namespace Eigen; NNSearchF* nns NNSearchF::createKDTreeLinearHeap(M); const int VectorXi indices(K); VectorXf dists2(K); nns->knn(q, indices, dists2, Eigen (refering software, math) matrix (column major, float) Eigen vector (float). Note stay alive libnabo, results knn undefined. results indices dists2 Eigen vectors indices squared distances refering columns examples/trivial.cpp compilable version examples/usage.cpp slightly complex involving multi-point queries. Running doc build directory generate browsable documentation doc/html. main page doc/html/index.html detailed overview usage libnabo. find complete CMake integration examples/libnabo-cmake-example link library. Python bindings libnabo includes python bindings compiled Python resulting module called pynabo, python/test.py. find information docstring-based documentation: python "import pynabo; help(pynabo.NearestNeighbourSearch)" Building Python bindings generated Python Python version interpreter building bindings, set PYTHON_VERSION_MAJOR PYTHON_VERSION_MINOR variables. Python installed, CMake generate Python bindings command. cmake -DPYTHON_VERSION_MAJOR=3 -DPYTHON_VERSION_MINOR=5 Debian-based distributions -DPYTHON_DEB_INSTALL_TARGET option enabled. description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.
The linux_networking package
sdsdddsdsds

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.
A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.
The lms1xx package contains a basic ROS driver for the SICK LMS1xx line of LIDARs.
readme: LMS1xx ROS driver SICK LMS1xx family laser scanners. Originally RCPRG.

description:
The marker_msgs package contains messages usable to setup a marker/fiducial system. 
    The package distinguishes between two types of messages. 
    First messages to describe the properties of a marker/fiducial detection system and the detected markers. 
    Secondly messages used to represent a map of markers/features with covariances as it would be produced by a SLAM system or published by a map server for self-localization.

description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting demo odometry Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

Overview interfaces MoveGroupInterface move arm move_group action. PlanningSceneInterface add/remove collision attached objects. set colors objects. PickPlaceInterface interface pick place actions. MoveGroupInterface MoveGroupInterface easy import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") MoveGroupInterface("planning_group_name", "base_link") values base_link, planning group called “planning_group_name”. PlanningSceneInterface PlanningSceneInterface easily insert remove objects MoveIt! planning scene. Starting version module newer service-based approach apply planning scene updates, robust publishing messages topics asynchronously. import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") create planning scene interface, provide root link PlanningSceneInterface("base_link") add cube size, base_link frame p.addCube("my_cube", remove cube p.removeCollisionObject("my_cube") reason prefer service, simply set “use_service” false add/remove calls. Calling waitForSync republish messages needed synchronize planning scene script MoveIt. p.addCube("my_cube", use_service=False) p.addCube("my_other_cube", use_service=False) p.waitForSync() PickPlaceInterface import rospy moveit_python import moveit_msgs.msg import Grasp, PlaceLocation rospy.init_node("moveit_py") provide arm group gripper group names takes parameter "plan_only" defaults False PickPlaceInterface("arm", "gripper") Grasp() fill setup object named object_name PlanningSceneInterface p.pickup("object_name", support_name "supporting_surface") PlaceLocation() fill p.place("object_name" goal_is_eef True, support_name "supporting_surface") Migration moveit_utils GraspingInterface renamed PickPlaceInterface pick/place functions return entire action result, moveit_utils returned error_code. access error code returned, result.error_code.val ObjectManager renamed PlanningSceneInterface remove function removeCollisionObject removeAttachedObject ArmInterface renamed MoveGroupInterface

description: Node for using a video file as video topic source.
readme: movie_publisher package tools movie files ROS (playback, conversion bag files handles file formats system installation ffmpeg decode. Important: package meant work moviepy. due packaging issues, moviepy installed automatically dependency. fallback OpenCV, worse. install moviepy manually calling: sudo pip install moviepy rosdep install python-moviepy-pip Main tools movie_publisher_node: ROS node serves video file video topic source (sensor_msgs/Image friends). movie_publisher.launch: launch file convenient usage node. starting image_transport/republish node converts video stream raw compressed/theora. mode, convert video files bagfiles batch. movie_to_bag: batch script takes video input transforms bag file. add_movie_to_bag: batch script adds video topic existing bag file. Helper tools fix_bag_timestamps: batch script rewrites bag files message publication time message header. generate bagfile data high speed reprocess suitable publication timestamps. merge.py: copy srv_tools/merge.py indigo. movie_publisher_node node run backends moviepy opencv. moviepy strongly recommended, ffmpeg, versatile efficient. set backend param, autodetection run. Published topics movie (sensor_msgs/Image): published movie, raw format. Node-private parameters: movie_file (string, required): Path movie play. format ffmpeg decode. fps (float, optional): set, playback FPS (speeding up/slowing movie). start (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback start time. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set duration. (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback stop time affected start). expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start duration. duration (float|tuple|string, optional): set, playback duration. set, duration counted clip, duration start clip. expressed seconds (min, sec), (hour, min, sec), string: set start loop (bool, default False): loop movie node shut Exludes (bool, default False): True, movie processed published quickly waiting real time. timestamps resulting messages act “real-world-like” FPS means frames’ timestamps sec set fake_time_start timestamps non-zero time. Excludes loop. playback_rate (float, optional): set number, mode play fast rate (set rate number lose messages, image_transport/republish). fake_time_start (float, default timestamp message. frame_id (string, default frame_id messages’ headers. spin_after_end (bool, default False): True, rospy.spin() called movie published. verbose (bool, default False): True, logs info frame played. wait_after_publisher_created (float, default workaround case give subscribers time publisher created. Tweak number missing start messages. publisher_queue_size (int, default mode, queue_size movie publisher. backend (string, default “moviepy”): backend reading video. moviepy opencv. moviepy selected opencv support codecs). ffmpeg (string, default nonempty, specifies (absolute) path ffmpeg binary movie_publisher.launch launch file takes arguments node’s parameters. Additionally, takes arguments: transport (string, default 'raw'): Type image transport. launch file start image_transport/republish node publishes video encoded transport type. republished_topic_basename (string, default movie_$(arg transport)): Base topic serve republished messages. Full topic $(arg republished_topic_basename)/$(arg transport) $(arg republished_topic_basename) case raw transport). base topic movie_publisher_node subscribes (movie default). movie_to_bag Convert movie file bag file video topic. Bash script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline ROS param server. Node-private parameters: movie (string): Path source movie file. bag (string): Path result stored. topic (string): movie’s topic bag file overwrite_bag (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing bag file, exits error bag exists. tmp_bag (string, default /tmp/movie.bag): Path temporary bag file stored. transport (string, default compressed): image transport encoding image. raw, compressed, theora installed image transport. Usage Call script commandline setting node-private parameters, pass arg:=value arguments relayed movie_publisher.launch pass arguments collide node-private parameters script movie_file). rosrun movie_publisher movie_to_bag _movie:=movie.mp4 _bag:=movie.bag _topic:="/movie" start:=5 fake_time_start:=1548323340.24 add_movie_to_bag Add movie file existing bagfile topic. Bash script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline ROS param server. Node-private parameters: movie (string): Path source movie file. bag_in (string): Path source bag file. bag_out (string, default: output.bag): Path result stored. topic (string): movie’s topic bag file. movie_delay (int, default Delay seconds) movie file bag file start. negative. overwrite_out_bag (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing bag_out file, exits error bag_out exists. bag_tmp (string, default /tmp/movie_add_to.bag): Path temporary bag file stored. transport (string, default compressed): image transport encoding image. raw, compressed, theora installed image transport. Usage Call script commandline setting node-private parameters, pass arg:=value arguments relayed movie_publisher.launch pass arguments collide node-private parameters script movie_file). rosrun movie_publisher add_movie_to_bag _movie:=movie.mp4 _bag_in:=movie_in.bag _bag_out:=movie_out.bag _topic:="/movie" start:=5 movie_delay:=-1 fix_bag_timestamps in_bag messages header field sets publication time time stored header.stamp delay. topics set, works messages listed topics. Reading timestamps supported. Python script ROS node-like API pass parameters _param:=value commandline. Node-private parameters: in_bag (string): Path source bag file. out_bag (string): Path result stored. topics (string, default: nonempty, rewrite timestamps listed topics. Pass comma-separated list topics. delay (int, default Delay seconds) added timestamp read header. negative. overwrite_existing (bool, default false): true, overwrites existing out_bag file, exits error out_bag exists.

description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.

authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com
