description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent
        to a mobile base.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.
Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.

description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.

description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs package contains various actions and messages that are used in the pr2_gripper_sensor* packages. The structure of the API used by pr2_gripper_sensor_action, and pr2_gripper_sensor_controller packages is as follows: 

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

  control loop.

description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

description:
Contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).
readme: realtime_tools ros_control documentation ros.org Build Status

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

description:
This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
	to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
	The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
	the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
	separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional jBrgment </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface
A ROS dependent wrapper for

The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.
This package contains the source code for testing and comparing trac_ik

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

readme: urg_stamped Precisely accurately stamped URG driver ROS Background Algorithm 2D-LIDAR URG series resolution timestamp exclusive clock synchronization mode. hard compensate clock drift measurement resolution timestamp high-speed motion sensor. urg_stamped estimates sub-millisecond algorithm: Select algorithms based sensor model UTM: UTM-30LX-EW UST(UUST1): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Unfixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version UST(UUST2-Fixed): UST-*LX, UST-*LC firmware version Determine sensor internal clock state (clock offset gain) command (Triggered timer default) UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed): models respond command expected SCIP2 protocol) Observe sub-millisecond clock offset finding increment millisecond resolution sensor timestamp Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset UST(UUST2-Unfixed): model responds command frame breaks SCIP2’s time synchronization logic) Request command times timing Filter responses large delay Collect sensor response timings synchronized sensor timestamp increment Observe sub-millisecond clock offset based sensor response timings sensor timestamps Observe clock gain multiple observations clock offset Determine scan origin time interval UTM: (UTM sends scan data scan finished) Observe scan timing based scan data arrival time UST: (UST sends scan data frame scan finished) Find sensor scan timestamp jitter Observe scan origin time scan interval scan timestamp jitter Calculate sub-millisecond scan timestamp based observed scan origin time scan interval LaserScan data stopped sensor internal clock estimation takes UTM/UST(UUST1)/UST(UUST2-Fixed) UST(UUST2-Unfixed). avoid stopping sensors multi-sensor configuration, urg_stamped automatically adjusts timing sensor internal clock estimation based messages urg_stamped_sync_start topic. Usages Topics major parameters designed compatible urg_node. Published Topics scan (sensor_msgs::LaserScan) Parameters urg_node compatible parameters ip_address (string): device address ip_port (int): device TCP/IP port frame_id (string): frame_id published scans publish_intensity (bool): fill intensity field true error_limit (int): reset sensor exit errors occur count urg_stamped specific parameters clock_estim_interval (double): sensor internal clock state estimation interval seconds (dropping scans estimation) fallback_on_continuous_scan_drop (int): fallback naive accuracy timestamp failed estimate sub-millisecond timestamp Limitations Timestamp estimation designed sensors connected ethernet interface. Tested sensor models: UTM-30LX-EW UST-05LX UST-20LX UST-30LC UUST2 model UST series (firmware version takes longer time perform time synchronization due sensor’s behavior. scans dropped due clock synchronization delay estimation. Comparison urg_node Configurations UTM-30LX-EWs mounted velocity controlled turntable, shown reconstruct point cloud scans. accuracy timestamp affects offset precision timestamp affects distribution pointcloud. The video_stream_opencv package contains a node to publish a video stream (the protocols that
    opencv supports are supported, including rtsp, webcams on /dev/video and video files) in ROS image topics, it supports camera info and basic image flipping (horizontal, vertical or both) capabilities, also adjusting publishing rate.

Usability input OpenCV system accepts, Video devices linux /dev/videoX, USB webcams appearing /dev/video0 Video streamings, rtsp://wowzaec2demo.streamlock.net/vod/mp4:BigBuckBunny_115k.mov Video files, play, myvideo.avi Parameters Note important behaviour node: set_camera_fps: set OpenCV parameter CV_CAP_PROP_FPS amount fps, camera video streams video files. buffer_queue_size: set size buffer images read capturing device. read fast thread) device store buffer images. main thread consumes buffer. shortest delay/lag set don’t lose images set higher. fps: effective rate image topic publish, effective fps camera throttle publication dropping frames needed. latest image published camera, set buffer_queue_size set_camera_fps max camera fps max. publish images (don’t drop don’t mind delay real time), set buffer_queue_size big case set_camera_fps fps FPS rest parameters explained, pretty explanatory: camera_name: node ros graph topics hang /camera_name/. video_stream_provider: number /dev/videoX device, /dev/video0. string path video file, /home/user/Videos/myvideo.avi url video stream rtsp://wowzaec2demo.streamlock.net/vod/mp4:BigBuckBunny_115k.mov http://10.68.0.6/mjpg/video.mjpg. frame_id: frame_id published Header messages. camera_info_url: camera info loading, care “file:///” start "file:///$(find your_camera_package)/config/your_camera.yaml". flip_horizontal: flip horizontally image (mirror flip_vertical: flip vertically image (upside loop_videofile: provider video file, enable loop playback. width height: force width height capturing device means forcing. start_frame: provider video file, set start frame video. stop_frame: provider video file, set stop frame video. means video. setting stop_frame start_frame publish single frame rate. Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode Flash code pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode Flash code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (indigo-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone indigo-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current

description: The geonav_transform package
readme: geonav_transform goal package simplify integration accurate/precise geographic navigation information (typically sensor) ROS localization navigation workflows. ends, geonav_tranform_node perform functions: Takes incoming Odometry messages, typically sensor, geographic position sensor-frame orientation velocities. (Note, nice message specifies type input Odometry bit hack.) Tranforms message Odometry message express information frames utm odom Broadcasts tranforms utm->odom odom->base_link use-case motivated project integrating sensors provide GPS-aided INS solution microstrain, advanced navigation, Xsens, situation analogous ekf/ukf node robot_localization package fuse IMU GPS information, case processing sensor. purpose package integration type sensor ROS navigation stack. Parameters ~datum: origin local “odom” frame element array [Latitude, Logitude, Altitude]. Lat/Lon decimal degrees; altitude meters. Default ~frequency: frequency broadcasting tranforms. Odometry messages published rate incoming Odometry messages. Default ~broadcast_utm2odom_transform: broadcast utm->odom tranform. Default True. ~broadcast_odom2base_transform: broadcast odom->base_link tranform. Default True. ~zero_altitude ~base_link_frame_id: Default “base_link” ~odom_frame_id: Default “odom” ~utm_frame_id: Default “utm” Subscribed Topics /odometry/nav: nav_msgs/Odometry message geographic position velocity data. message organized header.frame_id child_frame_id values pose.pose.position Latitude [dec. degrees] Longitude [dec. degrees] Altitude pose.pose.orientation base_link relative fixed ENU coordinate frame ~orientation_ned parameter set true, node convert orientation NED ENU. assuming orientation true magnetic). Typically magnetic declination set internal sensor providing information. pose.covariance expressed meters position radians orientation (REP-103) twist.twist.linear/angular velocity base_link frame twist.covariance expressed rad/s. Published Topics message published rate incoming /odometry/nav messages. Consistent navigation stack robot_localization, Odometry messages position pose information relative fixed frame (utm odom, reported header.frame_id). velocity (twist) information relative mobile frame (base_link, reported .child_frame). /odometry/odom: nav_msgs/Odometry message local odom frame (relative datum) /odometry/utm: nav_msgs/Odometry message UTM frame published static tranform: http://wiki.ros.org/tf2_ros http://wiki.ros.org/tf2/Tutorials/Writing%20a%20tf2%20static%20broadcaster%20(C%2B%2B) Published Transforms utm->odom odom->base_link Frames utm: global UTM coordinate frame. origin frame UTM zone determined datum parameter odom: local, fixed odom frame orgin datum parameter. assumed orientation UTM odom frame. general simplifies implementation, usage interpretation. base_link: mobile frame typically coincides sensor frame.

hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

The hector_worldmodel stack helps to collect and fuse information about objects in the world.
    It is used by Team Hector Darmstadt to localize victims and QR codes in the Robocup Rescue
    scenario. See

hector_worldmodel_geotiff_plugins contains a plugin to visualize worldmodel objects (e.g. victims in a
     Search and Rescue scenario) in a geotiff map generated by

hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

This is a programming framework to facilitate application development involving robot swarms. It makes coding for swarms much easier by providing an adequate swarm-level abstraction, as well as tools for swarm management, various communication mechanisms and so on. It also provides essential data structures, such as Neighbor, Swarm, and Virtual Stigmergy, to the user. Most importantly, it is completely compatible with ROS Indigo and presented in the form of a C++ library, which means that all resources in the ROS ecosystem are still available to the user. It is currently  extensible to Opensplice DDS.
authors: Xuefeng Chang, Zhongxuan Cai, Yanzhen Wang, Xiaodong Yi

Demo videos Build matrix status Distro Build dev Build releases Stable version ROS Noetic amd64 arm64 armhf ROS Humble amd64 arm64 ROS Jazzy amd64 arm64 ROS Rolling amd64 arm64 EOL distro Stable version ROS Melodic ROS Foxy ROS Iron Docs Main documentation site clone repository, remember checkout git submodules git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mvsim.git --recursive Launch demos steps Standalone: mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_warehouse.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_2robots.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/test_mesh.world.xml ROS roslaunch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch ROS ros2 launch mvsim demo_warehouse.launch.py ros2 launch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch.py Main features Lightweight memory, CPU library requirements. Fully configurable .xml “world” files. Headless mode, suitable dockerized environments. World maps: Occupancy gridmaps: input images MRPT binary maps icp-slam, rbpf-slam, Elevation meshes. Vehicle models: Differential driven wheel drive). Ackermann steering (kinematic dynamic steering, mechanical drive models). Ackermann steering mechanical differentials full grade. Sensors: Lidars: Robots bodies, RGB cameras Depth cameras Interface vehicles: Custom Python interface, ROS. Choose Raw access forces motor torques. Twist commands internal controllers).

description: myahrs_driver is a driver package for the WITHROBOT's myAHRS+. The myAHRS+ is a low cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) with USB/UART/I2C interface. The myAHRS+ board contains a 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, a 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer and a 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. The driver should also work with USB port.
readme: myahrs_driver Overview driver package WITHROBOT’s myAHRS+ http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs lilliputdirect http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 myAHRS+ cost high performance AHRS(Attitude Heading Reference System) USB/UART/I2C interface. myAHRS+ board 3-axis 16-bit gyroscope, 3-axis 16-bit accelerometer 3-axis 13-bit magnetometer. driver work USB port. Axes Convention myAHRS+ board NED type. myahrs_driver contained package converts frame conventions ROS east north (ENU) convention hand rule) publishing msgs. driver coordinate frame http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#axis-orientation information. left NED type IMU: x-north, y-east, z-down, relative magnetic north. ENU type IMU: x-east, y-north, z-up, relative magnetic north. Original Source original source support ROS) maintained github tutorials wiki page. visualization test 3D-box included original source. package myAHRS+ SDK https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus Video visualization demonstration RViz. Installation Install package: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-myahrs-driver Install package github: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/robotpilot/myahrs_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Run Run driver rosrun myahrs_driver myahrs_driver _port:=/dev/ttyACM0 roslaunch myahrs_driver myahrs_driver.launch Nodes Official ROS documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/myahrs_driver Communication Protocol Manual Forum myAHRS+ protocol here(https://github.com/withrobot/myAHRS_plus/tree/master/tutorial). Forum myAHRS+ user here(http://forum.odroid.com/viewforum.php?f=109). References References myAHRS+ board http://www.withrobot.com/myahrs_plus_en/ http://www.withrobot.com/?wpdmact=process&did=MTE4LmhvdGxpbms= https://github.com/robotpilot/myAHRS_plus http://www.hardkernel.com/main/products/prdt_info.php?g_code=G141464363369 http://www.lilliputdirect.com/odroid-myahrs References convention axes unit http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0003.html http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html https://github.com/paulbovbel/rep/blob/master/rep-0145.rst References similar IMU packages http://wiki.ros.org/um6 http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof https://github.com/KristofRobot/razor_imu_9dof http://docs.ros.org/api/sensor_msgs/html/msg/Imu.html

COLLADA 1.5 Robot Models Repository

    This repository is associated with the Open Robotics Automation Virtual Environment (OpenRAVE). The open and view them, install OpenRAVE and execute:
    
    openrave XXX.zae
    
    The robots are augmented with information as described by the "OpenRAVE" profile here:
    http://openrave.programmingvision.com/index.php/Started:COLLADA
    
    *.zae files are zip archives which contain the raw collada 1.5 xml (dae).
In order to use, please add the following line in your bashrc:
Bindings between openrave's controller interface and ROS's action lib interface for controlling robots.

Package uses openrave planning and the robot_calibration stacks to calibrate a robot's sensors. The calibration only depends on the openrave/collada descriptions, therefore it is meant to be a relatively simple way of calibrating new robots with one-click.
Uses ROS to parallelize the database generation processes inside OpenRAVE.
Contains ROS messages and services that are commonly used in the openrave_planning stack.

This stack offers OpenRAVE-related bindings.
    Maintained by the JSK lab at University of Tokyo.

Contains messages, services, and OpenRAVE clients to interface to robot hardware.

    - librobot_control - openrave plugin for ros client to talk to robot services
    - openravecontroller.h - base class for robot server using services
    - tfsender - given joint positions of the robot, sends the correct tf frames

    For more information on how to use this inside the openrave framework, see:
    http://openrave.programmingvision.com/wiki/index.php/ROS:ControllingRobots
Utilities for filtering the robot out of sensor data

description: The pioneer_teleop package provides teleoperation using keyboard, sockets or command line for the Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.).
readme: Pionner_teleop ROS package providing scripts teleoperation keyboard, sockets command line. package compatible robot ROS ecosystem, originally implemented Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur case robot, read robot Pionner-compatible?” section Note: package tested ROS Kinetic Kame Installation Dependencies install pioneer_bringup package, https://github.com/amine_horseman/pionner_bringup.git Source Download clone repository caktin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/amineHorseman/pioneer_teleop.git rosdep install pioneer_teleop Build catkin packages source ~/catkin_ws catkin_make python scripts executable ~/catkin_ws/src/pioneer_teleop/nodes sudo chmod Usage ##Teleop Modes package, ways teleoperation: Keyboard teleoperation: roslaunch pioneer_teleop keyboard_teleop.launch control robot motors keyboard arrows, increase decrease speed, stop version keyboard teleoperation command: roslaunch pioneer_teleop discrete_keyboard_teleop.launch case, robot moves small period time seconds default) stops. Sockets teleoperation: Controls robot remotely throught socket commands move robot web interface internet/LAN). roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch expected commands “left” default, script listens port robot moves seconds. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _port:=12345 _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 Command line teleoperation move robot command line throught terminal ssh roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_commandline.launch _direction:=forward expected commands (_direction argument) “left” default, robot moves seconds speed. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _direction:=backward _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 robot Pionner-compatible? package compatible robot ROS long velocity commands published /cmd_vel topic section). modify .launch scripts remove pionner_bringup call, execute python scripts located /nodes folder. issues Velocity command topic default, scripts publish velocity commands /cmd_vel topic. case velocity commands topic Pionner-compatible robots, remap velocity topic /cmd_vel change topic python scripts /nodes folder issues report problem issues panel TODO Feel free contribute repository Test package Ros Kinetic Kame Add joystick_teleop mode Add xbox360_teleop mode Add web page socket_teleop

description: Messages, serivices and actions for SawYer roch
Metapackage for SawYer roch robot software

A controller ensuring the safe operation of roch.

    The SafetyController keeps track of ult, psd and cliff events. In case of the first three,
    roch is commanded to move back. 
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (cliff, psd and ult etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!
    Refrence with yujinrobot' kobuki.

Ult/psd/cliff to pointcloud:
    Publish Ult, cliff, and psd sensors events as points in a pointcloud, so navistack can use them
    for poor-man navigation. Implemented intended to run together with roch_base.

description: Rocon test framework (i.e. multi-launch rostest framework).
readme: Rocon Tests Overview multimaster unit testing framework. rostest applied higher level Syntax, calling execution identical main difference multimaster launcher notes rocon_launch) regular ros launcher. rocon_test: command line executor multimaster tests. add_rocon_test: cmake macro including catkin build runs. Launchers sources typical rocon launcher rocon_test. <concert> <launch package="rocon_test" name="talker.launch" port="11311"/> <launch package="rocon_test" name="listener.launch" port="11312"/> </concert> individual launcher regular ros launcher, tests included. tests listener: <launch> <test test-name="listener" pkg="rocon_test" type="listener.py"/> <node name="latched_talker" pkg="rocon_test" type="talker.py" output="screen"/> </launch> Executing rocon_test rocon_test chatter.test --screen --text_mode Text mode full rocon_test output (setup, teardown notifications build directory /opt/rocon/build, find results test /opt/rocon/build/test_results/rocon_test/rostest-test_chatter.xml output test /opt/rocon/build/test_results/rocon_test/rosunit-listener.xml. Alternatively catkin_make yujin_make run tests, catch won’t run parallel roscore conflicts, call: yujin_make --run_tests examples rocon_gateway_tests.

description: Converts between Python dictionaries and JSON to rospy messages.
readme: rospy_message_converter Rospy_message_converter lightweight ROS package Python library convert Python dictionaries JSON messages rospy messages, vice versa. ROS ROS branches ROS users master branch. ROS users branch distro (foxy/galactic/humble/rolling/…). Usage Convert dictionary ROS message rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String dictionary 'data': 'Howdy' message message_converter.convert_dictionary_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', dictionary) Convert ROS message dictionary rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Howdy') dictionary message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_dictionary(message) Convert JSON ROS message rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String json_str '{"data": "Hello"}' message json_message_converter.convert_json_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', json_str) Convert ROS message JSON rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Hello') json_str json_message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_json(message) Test run tests: catkin_make test pre-commit Formatting Checks repo pre-commit check runs locally set run automatically commit install, pip: pip3 install --user pre-commit run files repo manually: pre-commit run run pre-commit automatically committing local repo, install git hooks: pre-commit install License Project released BSD license. description: The rtt_dynamic_reconfigure package
readme: rtt_dynamic_reconfigure package manipulate properties Orocos RTT component ROS dynamic_reconfigure interface. Dynamic reconfigure combination ROS topics, services, parameter server enable quick reconfiguration parameters network. Usage automatically generated config easiest rtt_dynamic_reconfigure special AutoConfig config type. This package provides an RTT plugin to access different time measurements on a realtime host.
readme: RTT ROSClock package helpful functions measuring time sources realtime operating system. running Orocos components ROS network, important time synchronized machines. standard RTT::TimeService calls query time, tens seconds NTP-corrected time timestamped messages published ROS dramatically delayed. avoid problems staying realtime-safe portable), rtt_rosclock::host_now() function ROS time structure CLOCK_HOST_REALTIME realtime time source Xenomai system. gnu/linux system, call return result standard ros::Time::now() function. host_now() broadcasting ROS Header Stamps rtt_rosclock::host_now() function time source ROS header timestamps time broadcast ROS messages machines processes. assumed /clock topic active, ROS messages broadcasted based time source. compiled Xenomai running simulation mode, function return NTP-synchronized clock time CLOCK_HOST_REALTIME clock source. Note supported Xenomai compiled Xenomai running simulation mode, pass-through ros::Time::now(). running simulation mode, simulation clock, based ROS /clock topic. pass-through rtt_rosclock::rtt_now(). RTT Services Clock Service “rosclock” RTT service sub-service global “ros” service called “ros.clock” operations: ros::Time ros.clock.host_now(void) ROS time structure NTP-adjusted realtime clock sim clock. ros::Time ros.clock.host_wall_now(void) ROS time structure NTP-adjusted realtime clock wall clock. ros::Time ros.clock.rtt_now(void) ROS time structure RTT clock sim clock. ros::Time ros.clock.rtt_wall_now(void) ROS time structure RTT wall clock. RTT::Seconds ros.clock.host_offset_from_rtt(void) differences RTT wall clock source NTP-adjusted realtime clock source. API calls rtt_rosclock/rtt_rosclock.h header: ros::Time rtt_rosclock::host_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::host_wall_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::rtt_now(void) ros::Time rtt_rosclock::rtt_wall_now(void) RTT::Seconds rtt_rosclock::host_offset_from_rtt(void) Simulation Clock Activity package RTT SimClockActivity periodically execute task subject progression simulated clock source. clock source ROS /clock topic exists /use_sim_time parameter set true, manual time update in-process source. simulation clock activity implemented classes: SimClockActivity actual activity executes task. SimClockActivityManager singleton class responsible coordinating execution SimClockActivity periodically (subject minimum desired periods). SimClockThread singleton thread responsible acutal updates RTT::os::TimeService optinally subscribing ROS messages /clock topic. default, SimClockThread running, started, override normal RTT TimeService update based update rate SimClockThread clock source. Usage SimClockActivity similarly RTT::PeriodicActivity. rtt_rosclock service plugin loaded, set number tasks’ activities SimClockActivities, set clock source, enable SimClockThread. import("rtt_ros"); ros.import("rtt_rosclock"); create components my_component_1.setPeriod(0.01); my_component_2.setPeriod(1.0); Set simulation clock activities ros.clock.setSimClockActivity(my_component_1); ros.clock.setSimClockActivity(my_component_2); Set SimClockThread ROS /clock topic time ros.clock.useROSClockTopic(); Start simulation clock thread override RTT clock ros.clock.enableSim();

description:
rtt_roscomm provides the necessary template files and cmake macros for
    automatic rtt typekit and transport generation from ROS msg files
readme: RTT ROS Communications Nomenclature Warning: “ROS Service” remote procedure call hapens ROS communication protocols “Orocos/RTT Service” grouping properties, functions, data ports. “ROS Services” satisfy similar role “Orocos/RTT Operations”. Contents package serves purposes. Orocos RTT Service publishing subscribing ROS topics Orocos RTT Services calling serving ROS services Orocos typekits ROS message primitive types template generating wrapper packages ROS .msg .srv files typekits .msg files transport plugin .msg files ros service proxy factories .srv files Plugins ROS Topics package global RTT service creating real-time-safe connections ROS topics Orocos RTT data ports. package Orocos connection policies: buffered unbuffered connections ROS topics. Publishing subscribing command, topic type inferred Orocos port type. Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. Note TOPIC_NAME prefixed tilde resolved process’s private namespace, similarly topic names resolved rospy. ROS Services package global RTT service task-scoped service facilitating communication ROS services. global service, rosservice_registry register factories creating proxies ROS service clients servers. task-scoped service ued bind RTT operations operation callers ROS services. general, users task-scoped RTT service, similarly rtt_rosparam service task-scoped RTT service rosservice operations: rosservice.connect(RTT_OPERATION_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_TYPE) Connect RTT operation ROS service. Note function RTT operation operation operation caller. RTT_OPERATION_NAME: task-scoped operation/operation caller provided/required services separated dots foo.bar.baz.op) ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) ROS_SERVICE_TYPE: full typename service std_srvs/Empty) rosservice.disconnect(ROS_SERVICE_NAME) Disconnects RTT operation operation caller ROS service server client.. ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) rosservice.disconnectAll() Disconnects RTT operations operation callers ROS service servers clients. global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. Usage Connecting Orocos Port ROS Topic Imports import("rtt_roscomm") Publish stream("my_component.my_output", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_output")) Subscribe stream("my_component.my_input", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_input")) set connections code: #include <rtt_roscomm/rtt_rostopic.h> Add port stream ROS topic this->ports()->addPort("my_port", my_port_); my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("my_ros_topic")); create privately-scoped component-scoped topic, Privately-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~my_private_ros_topic")); Component-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/COMPONENT_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~" this->getName() "/my_component_scoped_ros_topic")); Connecting RTT Operations ROS Services connect Orocos operation ROS service .ops script Orocos DeploymentComponent: Imports import("rtt_roscomm") import("rtt_std_srvs") Load application-specific component loadComponent("some_component_name","some_component_package::SomeComponent") Load rosservice RTT service components loadService("some_component_name","rosservice") Expose operation component ROS service some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_provided_service.some_operation", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Expose ROS service component some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_Required_service.some_operation_caller", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Making Package’s ROS .msg .srv Types Generally, create catkin package simply create_rtt_msgs script running: rosrun rtt_roscomm create_rtt_msgs my_msgs create package CMakeLists.txt package.xml: project(rtt_my_msgs) find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS rtt_roscomm) Generate typekits ros .msg files ros_generate_rtt_typekit(my_msgs) Generate plugin services my_msgs ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies(my_msgs) Call orocos_generate_package() export proper targets orocos_generate_package( DEPENDS my_msgs DEPENDS_TARGETS rtt_roscomm ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies() cmake function generate RTT plugin registers factories services named package plugin loaded. Design ROS Services rosservice_registry RTT service list ROS service clients servers RTT operations operationcallers, rosservice.connect operation, inspects argument Operation OperationCaller. RTT Operation, instantiate ROS service server wrapped RTT OperationCaller call operation. RTT OperationCaller, instantiate ROS service client wrapped RTT Operation called operation caller. required services wrapper operations operation callers created private rosservice service. description: This package provides an RTT service and service-requester for associating RTT component properties with ROS parameters
readme: RTT ROSParam package RTT service service-requester associating RTT component properties ROS parameters. Design RTT service enables setting/getting individual RTT properties RTT component to/from ROS parameter server, setting/getting properties component simultaneously. operations map operations roscpp library, store retrieved values RTT properties component. service requires RTT properties component match names ROS parameters, modulo namespace resolution policy Usage Service Interface Constants resolution policies): RELATIVE Relative resolution: ABSOLUTE Absolute resolution: PRIVATE Private resolution: COMPONENT Component resolution: “~COMPONENT_NAME/name” Operations (getting/setting params) Operations properties getAll() getAllComponentPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server COMPONENT namespace. getAllRelative() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server relative namespace. getAllAbsolute() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server absolute namespace. getAllPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server node’s private namespace. Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component “robot_description” ROS param global namespace (“/robot_description”) “publish_period” ROS param node’s private namespace (“~publish_period”) Include service requester interface #include <rtt_rosparam/rosparam.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: Storage RTT properties std::string robot_description_; double publish_period_; public: MyComponent::MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name) Add properties this->addProperty("robot_description",robot_description_); this->addProperty("publish_period",publish_period_); bool configureHook() bool all_params_found true; rosparam service requester boost::shared_ptr<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam> rosparam this->getProvider<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam>("rosparam"); parameters if(rosparam) ROS parameter "/robot_description" all_params_found rosparam->getAbsolute("robot_description"); ROS parameter "~publish_period" all_params_found rosparam->getPrivate("publish_priod"); return all_params_found; API documentation information.

description: This package contains the components of the rtt_tf package
readme: RTT package Orocos RTT component utilizing rigid body transform library Orocos. component service operations requesting broadcasting sing batch transforms. Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

Depending current version ROS, rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Quick Start Demo random shapes generated Rviz, launch Rviz: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo_rviz.launch start demo: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo.launch combined roslaunch file: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo_combined.launch Code API VisualTools Class Reference Usage assume helper functions class. functions assume publishing transforms world frame call /odom). description:
ROS driver package for the thingmagic USB pro RFID reader.  The driver creates a node named /rfid_detector_node and 
publish all detected tags on the topic /RFID_detections as custom RFID_Detection messages.  The RFID_Detection messages have 4
fields: 
  epc:  A string containing the RFID tag identifier detected
  antenna:  8-bit integer containing the antenna number of the detection
  read_count:  8-bit integer containing the number of detections during the collection period
  rssi:  8-bit integer containing the RSSI value of the detection 

Uses the python wrapper for ThingMagic's Mercury API located here: https://github.com/gotthardp/python-mercuryapi
readme: ROS driver ThingMagic USB Pro RFID detector Install Package Dependencies (clone repo install): ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://gitlab.com/chambana/thingmagic_usbpro.git ./thingmagic_usbpro/install_script.sh ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Launch Package: roslaunch thingmagic_usbpro rfid_detector.launch Settings settings configured editing file thingmagic_usbpro/launch/rfid_detector.launch Device port default /dev/ttyACM0 Device Region code default North America Message Publish Rate (max) default Petr Gotthard maintaing Mercury API Python Wrapper

description: TinySLAM ROS implementation
readme: TinySLAM implementation ROS Description package ROS implentation tinySLAM (https://openslam.org/tinyslam.html) simpliest lightweight SLAM methods. Current implementation compatible ROS Jade adds improvements original method: alternative model grid map cell track average stored effective values (probability occupied scan’s quality account); option compute probability cell occupied based laser beam splits cell original implementation constant Hardware Requirements Current implementation supposes robot odometry data laser scan measurements horizontally mounted fixed laser scanner. data ROS topics Subscribed topics). launch-file run configure tinySLAM node: roslaunch tiny_slam tinyslam_run.launch path:=[path dataset] Dataset BAG format (http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag). comment rosbag node launch file real-time data replace dataset player. Nodes tiny_slam tiny_slam node takes sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages odometry data topic builds map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) retrieved ROS topic /map. Subscribed Topics (tf/tfMessage) Transforms relate frames laser, base, odometry laser_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scans create map Published Topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data topic (updated periodically) Parameters ~cell_type (string, default: “avg”) Accepted values: “avg” modified cell model; “base” original cell model ~occupancy_estimator (string, default: “const”) Accepted values: “const” original occupancy estimator; “area” dynamic occupancy estimator ~skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (boolean, default: false) Determines handle range laser measurements: true ignore measurements, false process empty cells. ~base_occupied_prob (float, default: initial probability cell occupied probability laser scan data valid) ~base_empty_prob (float, default: initial probability cell empty probability range laser scan data valid) Required Transforms base_link odom_combined: odometry system driver mobile base) Transforms map robot_pose: current estimate robot’s pose map frame Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

description: The adhoc_communication package allows to exchange data over an ad-hoc network setup by robots running the corresponding node. The package allows you to exchange data over serveral roscores by wrapping transmitting the data to the destination and publishing the data on a predefined topic at the destination host. Routing is accomplished using the hostname of the robots.

The explorer package utilizes frontier based exploration for multi-robot systems. Beside frontier detection, coordinated and uncoordinated exploration strategies are available to select goal points. Coordinated exploration enhances robot distribution and reduces redundancy in exploration reducing exploration time.
Map merger is a package to merge several maps on the fly to one global map, if possible

description: The industrial_desktop package contains all "released" packages
Metapackage for interaction cursor functionality.
A demo 3D cursor interaction application.

A message API for implementing a 6-DOF interaction cursor.
    A minimal implementation has one node that handles the user device I/O and
    another node (e.g. a plugin in Rviz) that uses the messages to interact with
    a marker.

An Rviz plugin that implements the interaction_cursor_msgs API
    to interact with interactive_markers.

readme: jsk_magnistartup SetUp (Running commands time robot) fix ROS_HOSTNAME robot Edit /etc/ubiquity/env.sh #!/bin/sh #export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname).local export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname) export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://$ROS_HOSTNAME:11311 Setup pigpid.services https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar, wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joan2937/pigpio/master/util/pigpiod.service sudo pigpiod.service /etc/systemd/system sudo systemctl enable pigpiod.service sudo systemctl start pigpiod.service add jsk_magni.service Add /etc/systemd/system/jsk-magni-startup.service file [Unit] Requires=roscore.service PartOf=roscore.service After=magni-base.service [Service] Type=simple User=ubuntu ExecStart=/usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target Add /usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup #!/bin/bash function log() logger user.$1 log info "magni-base: workspace setup file /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash" source /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash log_path="/tmp" source /etc/ubiquity/env.sh log info "magni-base: Launching ROS_HOSTNAME=$ROS_HOSTNAME, ROS_IP=$ROS_IP, ROS_MASTER_URI=$ROS_MASTER_URI, ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path" Punch export ROS_HOME=$(echo ~ubuntu)/.ros export ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path roslaunch jsk_magni_startup magni_bringup.launch PID=$! log info "jsk-magni-startup: Started roslaunch background process, PID $PID, ROS_LOG_DIR=$ROS_LOG_DIR" echo "$PID" $log_path/jsk-magni-startup.pid wait "$PID" Enable service sudo systemctl enable jsk-magni-startup.service sudo systemctl start jsk-magni-startup.service Add latest codes catkin_ws Update catkin_ws/src git: local-name: demos uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/demos.git version: 52136f04c0f39fe6a1001a17e208e5ce5b4cda61 git: local-name: magni_robot uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/magni_robot version: 603cae184bcc59b96d59f2fee6029b22725c3c5c git: local-name: pi_sonar uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar version: b84458f909d5febd410b8cd68d552e9aecad685c

(Optional) Preparation Chinese demo move files /jsk_201504_miraikan/file /home/nao/audio_file Pepper’s log Pepper’s ssh nao@<Pepper's audio_file folder, /home/nao/. move files roscd jsk_201504_miraikan file scp <file nao@<Pepper IP>:/home/nao/audio_file/ execute languages roslaunch jsk_pepper_startup jsk_pepper_startup.launch roseus mirai-demo-2015-0413.l demo Japanese demo English demo :chi Chinese

description: jsk_pr2_startup
readme: jsk_pr2_startup setup rewrite /etc/ros/robot.launch rewrite /etc/ros/robot.launch <launch> Robot Description <param name="robot_description" textfile="/etc/ros/groovy/urdf/robot.xml" Robot Analyzer <rosparam command="load" file="$(find pr2_bringup)/config/pr2_analyzers.yaml" ns="diag_agg" Robot bringup <include file="$(find jsk_pr2_startup)/pr2_bringup.launch" <group> <remap from="/joy" to="/joy_org"/> <include file="$(find pr2_bringup)/pr2.launch" </group> Web include file="$(find webui)/webui.launch" Android app <include file="$(find local_app_manager)/app_manager.launch" <arg name="ROBOT_NAME" value="pr1012" <arg name="ROBOT_TYPE" value="pr2" </include> RobotWebTools <include file="$(find rwt_image_view)/launch/rwt_image_view.launch"/> kinect <include file="$(find jsk_pr2_startup)/jsk_pr2_sensors/kinect_head.launch"> <arg name="respawn" value="false" </include> <rosparam file="/etc/ros/robot.yaml"/> </launch> launch mongodb multiple users users unix group run mongod single owned group. mongod opens database files O_NOATIME flag open system call. Open O_NOATIME works UID completelly matchs caller priviledged (CAP_FOWNER) security reasons. launch mongodb shared database resouces, POSIX Capabilities Linux. Ubuntu sudo aptitude install libcap2-bin sudo setcap cap_fowner+ep /usr/bin/mongod Hark Microcone documentation Hark installation: http://www.hark.jp/wiki.cgi?page=HARK+Installation+Instructions hark jsk installation: https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty/blob/master/hark_jsk_plugins/INSTALL Microcone: http://www.hark.jp/wiki.cgi?page=SupportedHardware#p10 Bind rfcomm device binding rfcomm device, connect bluetooth device device file /dev/rfcomm1). rosserial bluetooth connection. detail, https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_robot/blob/master/jsk_robot_common/jsk_robot_startup/README.md#launchrfcomm_bindlaunch usage Save bluetooth device MAC address file /var/lib/robot/rfcomm_devices.yaml device1 address: XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX device2 address: YY:YY:YY:YY:YY:YY bind rfcomm devices. login root user ssh Assume bluetooth dongle plugged roslaunch jsk_pr2_startup pr2_rfcomm_bind.launch machine:=c2 check devices bound rfcomm, rfcomm command. ssh ssh rfcomm management pr2_rfcomm_bind.launch started automatically upstart. upstart config file /etc/upstart/jsk-rfcomm-bind.conf

description: jsk_unitree_startup
The unitreeeus package
EusLisp software developed and used by JSK at The University of Tokyo
readme: JSK EusLisp repository repository containts software devleoped JSK University Tokyo. started Install binary (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install Euslisp sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-euslisp Install Homebrew (OSX) brew install euslisp/jskeus/jskeus Playing Demo programs Motion generation irteusgl irteus/demo/demo.l type command, ‘(crank-motion)’ ‘(dual-arm-ik)’ Robots objects models irteusgl models/irt-all-robots.l "(make-all-robots)" irteusgl models/irt-all-objects.l "(make-all-objects)" started ROS users Install binaries (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository skip procdeure setup ROS system sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install pr2/euslisp programs sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-pr2eus Running demo programs Visualizinot robot rosrun euscollada pr2.sh real users set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP robot simulated command roscd pr2eus read joint state display euslisp viewer, print jacobian roslaunch ./pr2-read-state.launch sample program show posture euslisp, MOVE REAL ROBOT, CAUTION!!! roslaunch ./pr2-send-joints.launch script set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP source `rospack find jsk_tools`/src/bashrc.ros rossetrobot pr1012.jsk.com change environment rossetip roseus package http://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg repository ROS client library euslisp Support Trouble Shooting issue tracker support pull requests report bugs patches. Online Documents Japanese avilable) online manual PDF files Install euslisp source code (EXPERTS Installing dependent libraries Ubuntu users sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi gsfonts-x11 texlive-fonts-extra xfonts-100dpi-transcoded xfonts-75dpi-transcoded msttcorefonts Mac OSX users Homebrew brew install jpeg libpng mesalib-glw wget ’'’NOTE:’’’ EusLisp Library, Mac OSX mountain lion longer support install substitution XQuartz. Mac OSX lion older) users install latest XQuartz safety. XQuartz older installation EusLisp fail. install latest XQuartz.) Raspberry users debian(Raspbian “wheezy”) sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg62-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi additional install OpenGL raspberry sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-dri xserver-xorg-video-vesa xutils Cygwin(now working 32bit Packages listed intalled setup.exe. git, gcc-core, gcc-g++, libjpeg-devel, libpng-devel libGL-devel, libGLU-devel, libglut-devel, libX11-devel, libXext-devel xorg-server X-start-menu-icons startxwin.exe) mesa-demo glxinfo) bitmap fonts bitmap font packages -75dpi, -100dpi, -misc Downloading building EusLisp git clone http://github.com/euslisp/jskeus jskeus jskeus instructions add EUSDIR, ARCHDIR, PATH, LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable bashrc invoke euslisp program echo "source ~/bashrc.eus" ~/.bashrc Install ROS jsk-ros-pkg repository http://wiki.ros.org/indigo/Installation/Source detail sudo apt-get install python-rosdep python-rosinstall-generator python-wstool python-rosinstall build-essential mkdir ~/ros_catkin_ws/src ~/ros_catkin_ws/src wstool init src https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_pr2eus/master/.rosinstall wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_travis/master/rosdep-install.sh ./rosdep-install.sh catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Acknowledgment software repository based EusLisp language.
