libccd is library for collision detection between two convex shapes.
readme: libccd libccd library collision detection convex shapes. libccd implements variation Gilbert–Johnson–Keerthi algorithm Expand Polytope Algorithm (EPA) implements algorithm Minkowski Portal Refinement (MPR, a.k.a. XenoCollide) Game Programming Gems libccd open source library knowledge include MPR algorithm working space. library called mpr2d, implemented programming language, works space. libccd part ODE library ODE’s ./configure enable FCL library Willow Garage, Bullet3 library (https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3). implementation details GJK algorithm, http://www.win.tue.nl/~gino/solid/jgt98convex.pdf. Dependencies library based standard libraries. exception testsuites built top (https://github.com/danfis/cu) library licensed LGPL, testing depends libccd library distributed License libccd licensed OSI-approved 3-clause BSD License, text license distributed source code BSD-LICENSE file. file include license notice, rest considered licensed 3-clause BSD License. Compile Install libccd mechanisms compiling installing. simple Makefile, autotools, CMake. Makefile Directory src/ Makefile needed compilation installation: src/ install Library libccd default compiled double precision floating point numbers change options USE_SINGLE/USE_DOUBLE, USE_SINGLE=yes compile library single precision. Installation directory changed options PREFIX, INCLUDEDIR LIBDIR. info type Autotools libccd support autotools: Generate configure script ./bootstrap Create build/ directory: mkdir build build Run configure script: ../configure Run install: install configure script change libccd compiled installed, significant option –enable-double-precision enables double precision (single default case). CMake build mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" install build ninja: mkdir build build cmake Ninja ninja ninja install build tools generator. cmake Xcode cmake "Visual Studio compile double precision, set ENABLE_DOUBLE_PRECISION option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DENABLE_DOUBLE_PRECISION=ON install build libccd shared library, set BUILD_SHARED_LIBS option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON install build test suite, set BUILD_TESTING option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DBUILD_TESTING=ON test installation directory changed CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX variable: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/path/to/install install GJK Intersection Test section describes libccd testing convex objects intersects ‘yes/no’ test) Gilbert-Johnson-Keerthi (GJK) algorithm. Procedure simple similar usages library): Include <ccd/ccd.h> file. Implement support function specific shapes. Support function function returns furthest point object (shape) direction. Set ccd_t structure. Run ccdGJKIntersect() function desired objects. skeleton simple program: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function box void support(const void *obj, const ccd_vec3_t *dir, ccd_vec3_t *vec) assume obj_t user-defined structure holds info object case box: pos, quat dimensions box, position rotation) obj_t *obj (obj_t *)_obj; ccd_vec3_t dir; ccd_quat_t qinv; apply rotation direction vector ccdVec3Copy(&dir, _dir); ccdQuatInvert2(&qinv, &obj->quat); ccdQuatRotVec(&dir, &qinv); compute support point direction ccdVec3Set(v, ccdSign(ccdVec3X(&dir)) box->x CCD_REAL(0.5), ccdSign(ccdVec3Y(&dir)) box->y CCD_REAL(0.5), ccdSign(ccdVec3Z(&dir)) box->z CCD_REAL(0.5)); transform support point position rotation object ccdQuatRotVec(v, &obj->quat); ccdVec3Add(v, &obj->pos); int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.max_iterations maximal number iterations int intersect ccdGJKIntersect(obj1, obj2, &ccd); intersect holds true obj1 obj2 intersect, false GJK EPA Penetration Objects obtain penetration info intersection objects ccdGJKPenetration() function Procedure previous case: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.max_iterations maximal number iterations ccd.epa_tolerance maximal tolerance fro EPA part ccd_real_t depth; ccd_vec3_t dir, pos; int intersect ccdGJKPenetration(obj1, obj2, &ccd, &depth, &dir, &pos); intersect holds obj1 obj2 intersect, depth, dir pos stored penetration depth, direction separation vector position global coordinate system MPR Intersection Test libccd MPR Minkowski Portal Refinement algorithm testing objects intersects. Procedure similar GJK algorithm. Support function function returns center point center) object implemented: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case Center function returns center object void center(const void *_obj, ccd_vec3_t *center) obj_t *obj (obj_t *)_obj; ccdVec3Copy(center, &obj->pos); int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.center1 center; center function object ccd.center2 center; center function object ccd.mpr_tolerance maximal tolerance int intersect ccdMPRIntersect(obj1, obj2, &ccd); intersect holds true obj1 obj2 intersect, false MPR Penetration Objects MPR algorithm obtaining penetration info intersection objects equally easy previous case ccdMPRPenetration() function #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case Center function prevous case int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.center1 center; center function object ccd.center2 center; center function object ccd.mpr_tolerance maximal tolerance ccd_real_t depth; ccd_vec3_t dir, pos; int intersect ccdMPRPenetration(obj1, obj2, &ccd, &depth, &dir, &pos); intersect holds obj1 obj2 intersect, depth, dir pos stored penetration depth, direction separation vector position global coordinate system

description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz
readme: Official ROS Documentation extensive standard ROS-style version documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof Install Configure ROS Package Install dependencies visualization): sudo apt-get install python-visual Ubuntu Ubuntu install https://github.com/lebarsfa/visual/tree/bionic, https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof/issues/47 sudo apt-get install python3-pip python3-wxgtk4.0 pip3 install vpython Ubuntu Download code: ~/catkin_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof.git catkin_make visualization, Ubuntu src/razor_imu_9dof/nodes wget https://www.glowscript.org/docs/VPythonDocs/VPtoGS.py python3 VPtoGS.py Converted/display_3D_visualization.py display_3D_visualization.py Install Arduino firmware SPX-15846 DEV-16832 (OpenLog Artemis), follow instructions OLA_IMU_Basics.ino sample https://github.com/sparkfun/OpenLog_Artemis drivers https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/how-to-install-ch340-drivers, install SparkFun Apollo3 boards Arduino IDE https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-board-definitions-in-the-arduino-ide (add https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sparkfun/Arduino_Boards/master/IDE_Board_Manager/package_sparkfun_index.json File Preferences Additional Board Manager URLs) ensure select SparkFun Apollo3 SparkFun RedBoard Artemis ATP board install SparkFun ICM IMU Arduino library https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-an-arduino-library). SEN-14001 (9DoF Razor IMU follow instructions default firmware https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/9dof-razor-imu-m0-hookup-guide updated version SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library https://github.com/lebarsfa/SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library updated version default firmware https://github.com/lebarsfa/9DOF_Razor_IMU). Open src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino Arduino IDE. Note: modified version Peter Bartz’ original Arduino code https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs). version emits linear acceleration angular velocity data required ROS Imu message Select hardware uncommenting line src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino, HARDWARE OPTIONS Select hardware uncommenting line! //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10125" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10736" (HMC5883L magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DoF Razor IMU version "SEN-14001" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "SPX-15846" #define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "DEV-16832" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10183" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10321" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10724" (HMC5883L magnetometer) Upload Arduino sketch board Configure default configuration, razor_imu_9dof expects yaml config file my_razor.yaml USB port Calibration parameters examplerazor.yaml file Copy file my_razor.yaml roscd razor_imu_9dof/config razor.yaml my_razor.yaml edit my_razor.yaml needed Launch Publisher roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub.launch Publisher visualization: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-and-display.launch Publisher diagnostics: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-diags.launch visualization roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-display.launch Calibrate accuracy, follow tutorial calibrate sensors: http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof updated version Peter Bartz’s magnetometer calibration scripts https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs magnetometer_calibration directory. Update my_razor.yaml calibration parameters. Dynamic Reconfigure launched publisher launch commands listed dynamically reconfigure yaw calibration. Run: rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure Select imu_node. Change slider move calibration degrees. running visualization you’ll display jump calibration takes effect. intent feature tune alignment AHRS direction robot driving direction, determine AHRS reads degrees robot degrees shown GPS, tune calibration read compass-equivalent bore-sighting camera.

description:
High-level navigation application for the segbot allowing the segbot to
    approach and gothrough doors. The application can also be used to determine
    the segbot's logical location, as well as sense when a door in front of the
    robot is open or not.

Contains launch files for running the ROS navigation stack on the segbot
    using the eband_local_planner approach, as well as launch files for amcl
    and gmapping.

Contains sensor specific launch files and all the relevant filters that are
    applied on sensor data before being used by the segbot.
readme: Overview segbot_sensors package special-purpose drivers UTexas BWI segbots. ROS interfaces arduino_driver node reads serial messages Arduino board, converting data attached sensors ROS messages. Published topics battery0 (sensor_msgs/BatteryState) Main segbot battery voltage measurements. imu0 (sensor_msgs/Imu) Acceleration data inertial measurement unit. sonar (sensor_msgs/Range) Sonar ranges attached device frame. /diagnostics (diagnostic_msgs/DiagnosticArray) device diagnostics. Parameters ~baud (int, default: Baud rate Arduino serial port. ~port (string, default: /dev/ttyACM0) Serial port attached Arduino. Usage run driver node: rosrun segbot_sensors arduino_driver ranges_to_cloud node converts sonar Range messages point clouds. Subscribed topics sonar (sensor_msgs/Range) Sonar ranges attached device. Published topics sensor_ranges (sensor_msgs/PointCloud2) centered distance point sonar range. Usage run arduino_driver node: roslaunch segbot_sensors arduino.launch

description: Stereo Slam
readme: Stereo SLAM stereo_slam ROS node execute Simultaneous Localization Mapping (SLAM) stereo camera. algorithm designed tested underwater robotics. node based library graph optimization power libhaloc find loop closures graph nodes. keyframe multi-keyframe loop closing mechanism, based keypoint clustering, improve SLAM corrections feature-poor environments. action Specially designed underwater scenarios: Video: ORB-SLAM Stereo SLAM (underwater) videos… Video: Stereo SLAM reconstruction Video: Stereo SLAM UIB outdoor pond Installation (Ubuntu ROS Indigo) Install dependencies sudo apt-get install ros-<your ros distro>-libg2o sudo apt-get install libceres-dev setup stereo visual odometer viso2 fovis). Download code, save ROS workspace enviroment compile. roscd git clone https://github.com/srv/stereo_slam.git stereo_slam rosmake Parameters odom_topic Visual odometry topic (type nav_msgs::Odometry). camera_topic namespace stereo camera. (hard-coded) parameters include/constants.h set node parameters. Default parameters work. Run node run node launch file performance scale images width). <launch> <arg name="camera" default="/stereo"/> Run stereo image proc <node ns="$(arg camera)" pkg="stereo_image_proc" type="stereo_image_proc" name="stereo_image_proc" <node pkg="viso2_ros" type="stereo_odometer" name="stereo_odometer"> <remap from="stereo" to="$(arg camera)"/> <remap from="image" to="image_rect"/> </node> <node pkg="stereo_slam" type="localization" name="stereo_slam" output="screen"> <param name="odom_topic" value="/stereo_odometer/odometry"/> <param name="camera_topic" value="$(arg camera)"/> </node> </launch> Published Topics /stereo_slam/odometry vehicle pose (type nav_msgs::Odometry). /stereo_slam/graph_poses updated graph poses (type stereo_slam::GraphPoses). /stereo_slam/keyframes Number inserted keyframes (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/keypoints_clustering Image keypoint clusters (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings Image loop closing correspondences. Correspondences keyframe-to-multi-keyframe (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_queue Number keyframes waiting loop closing queue. monitor topic, check real-time performance: number grows indefinitely means system able process keyframes, scale images. (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/loop_closings Number loop closings (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/pointcloud pointcloud keyframe (type sensor_msgs::PointCloud2). /stereo_slam/tracking_overlap Image representation traking overlap. decide insert keyframe graph (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/camera_params optimized (calibrated) camera parameters loop closure (type stereo_slam::CameraParams). Saved data node stores data stereo_slam directory execution: haloc folder files needed libhaloc library, responsible loop closing detection. folder keyframes Stores left stereo image keyframe possibility drawing keypoint clustering image). loop_closures Stores images published topic /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings. pointclouds Stores pointclouds published topic /stereo_slam/pointcloud. Online graph viewer node python script visualize results stereo_slam execution: scripts/graph_viewer.py: usage: graph_viewer.py ground_truth_file visual_odometry_file graph_vertices_file graph_edges_file (automatically detect graph files): roscd stereo_slam ./scripts/graph_viewer.py odometry file recorded ros rostopic echo /your_odometry_topic/odometry odometry.txt ground truth file format odometry. Post processing node python script evaluate results stereo_slam execution finishes: scripts/slam_evaluation.py: usage: slam_evaluation.py ground_truth_file visual_odometry_file graph_vertices_file graph_edges_file script perform set operations order evaluate performance stereo_slam algorithm: Align curves (ground truth, visual_odometry graph vertices) origin. Compute average translation error. Plot error trajectory distance.

description: Dynamixel and 3mxl driver

This library offers an introspection mechanism for C/C++ value-types. I.e.
      it offers a way to represent types, and to manipulate in-memory values
      that are instances of those types.

      A Ruby binding is included, which gives a fast and transparent
      modification of C/C++ in-memory types from Ruby.

License: BSD Author: Markus Achtelik <markus.achtelik@mavt.ethz.ch> QUICK START roslaunch vicon_bridge vicon.launch set "datastream_hostport" parameter vicon computer's ip/hostname OPERATION vicon_bridge node initiates connection Vicon data source Nexus Tracker) DataStream API. parameter ~datastream_hostport set address port (joined colon) DataStream server machine, default port). parameter ~stream_mode call DataStream SetStreamMode method; valid values "ServerPush", "ClientPull". subjects segments recognized automatically published transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped ORIGIN CALIBRATION object created vicon tracker, places origin arbitrary object. Setting desired origin Tracker tedious, added origin calibration method. Place vehicle vicon origin set vicon calibration, measure/estimate height offset) desired origin floor. run rosrun vicon_bridge calibrate <subject <segment offset> Poses grabbed, averaged vehicle vicon's origin. calibration parameters stored parameter server ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose startup, node checks calibration parameters time running rviz observe correct Published topics: vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> publishes subjects/segments vicon/markers publishes labeled unlabeled markers. Labeled markers affected origin calibration Services: grab_vicon_pose grab poses subject/segment averages calibrate_segment calls origin calibration segment transforms vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> Parameters stream_mode mode connect DataStream server. The camera_handler package

The contact_handler package. It reports the dynamic-engine-reported contacts between
      all scene objects on a topic called "contacts".

      This package uses gazebo_msgs/ContactsState message type to report body contacts.
      This is because ROS doesn't have any standardized contact reporting messages.
      However, to have gazebo_msgs, you don't need to install whole Gazebo, you just need to
      install the single package ros-indigo-gazebo-msgs.

readme: EigenPy Versatile efficient Python bindings Numpy Eigen EigenPy open-source framework binding famous Eigen library Python Boost.Python. EigenPy full memory sharing Numpy Eigen, avoiding memory allocation full support Eigen::Ref avoiding memory allocation full support Eigen::Tensor module exposition Geometry module Eigen easy code prototyping standard matrix decomposion routines Eigen Cholesky decomposition (SVD decompositions added) full support SWIG objects full support runtime declaration Numpy scalar types extended API expose STL types Boost equivalents: optional types, std::pair, maps, variants… full support vectorization Python hold objects properly aligned memory) Installation installation EigenPy computer easy Linux/BSD, Mac Windows environments. Conda simply simple line: conda install eigenpy conda-forge Ubuntu easily install EigenPy binaries. Add robotpkg apt repository Add robotpkg source repository apt: sudo "echo 'deb [arch=amd64] http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/pub $(lsb_release robotpkg' /etc/apt/sources.list.d/robotpkg.list" Register authentication certificate robotpkg: curl http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/robotpkg.key sudo apt-key add run apt update fetch package descriptions: sudo apt-get update Install EigenPy installation EigenPy dependencies line: sudo apt install robotpkg-py35-eigenpy replaced Python work robotpkg-py36-eigenpy work Python Mac installation EigenPy Mac HomeBrew. register tap software repository. brew tap gepetto/homebrew-gepetto install EigenPy Python brew install eigenpy Build Build instruction Credits people involved development EigenPy: Justin Carpentier (Inria): main developer manager project Nicolas Mansard (LAAS-CNRS): initial project instructor Wolfgang Merkt (University Edinburgh): ROS integration support Sean Yen (Microsoft): Windows integration Loïc Estève (Inria): Conda integration Wilson Jallet (Inria/LAAS-CNRS): core developer Joris Vaillant (Inria): core developer manager project part development EigenPy, feel free add contribution Acknowledgments development EigenPy supported Gepetto team @LAAS-CNRS Willow team @INRIA.

A ROS package for face recognition in video stream.
Face recognition is performed using Eigenfaces (also called "Principal Component Analysis" or PCA) by utilizing the c++ source code provided by Shervin Emami (http://www.shervinemami.info/faceRecognition.html)
readme: Copyright Pouyan Ziafati, University Luxembourg image processing face recognition functionalities utilizing Shervin Emami’s source code face recognition (http://www.shervinemami.info/faceRecognition.html). License: Attribution-NonCommercial Unported (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/3.0/) face_recognition package ROS face_recognition ROS package simple actionlib server interface performing face recognition functionalities video stream. Instalation instalation process catkin (ROS Groovy newer version) Assuming catkin workspace ~/catkin_ws, replace ~/catkin_ws location. assumes you’re running Bash shell, you’re running Zsh, source setup.zsh file. ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/procrob/procrob_functional.git --branch catkin ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash FaceRecognitionGoal message includes fields: int order_id string order_argument face recognition actionlib server accepts goals: order_id (Recognize Goal acknowledge face recognized video stream. face recognized confidence higher desirable confidence threshold, person confidence client result. order_id (Recognize Continuously) Goal continuously recognize faces video stream. face recognized confidence higher desirable confidence threshold confidence client feedback. goal persuaded infinite time canceled preempted goal. order_id order_argument person_name (Add face images) Goal acquire training images person. video stream processed detecting face saved training image person. process continued desired number training images person acquired. person “order_argument” order_id (Train) database (re)trained training images order_id (Exit) program exits. Subscribed Topic: /camera/image_raw video stream (standard ROS image transport) Parameters: confidence_value (double, default face recognized confidence higher confidence_value threshold accepted valid. show_screen_flag (boolean, default true) output screen shown add_face_number (int, default parameter add_face_images goal determines number training images person acquired video stream Face Recognition Works: Details http://www.shervinemami.info/faceRecognition.html persons images faces train stored train.txt file. file directory execute program. train.txt file desirable format train.txt (Note: person numbers start spaces special characters allowed persons’ names). program trains train.txt file stores eigenfaces data facedata.xml file. face_recognition actionlib server executed (Fserver), program load data facedata.xml file exists, learn training images stored train.txt add training data video stream add_face_images goal (order_id Note: program retrains (order_id content facedata.xml disregarded program trains based train.txt file. Tutorial: demonstration purposes actionlib client face_recognition simple actionlib server client subscribes face_recognition/FRClientGoal messages. FRClientGoal message order_id order_argument goal executed face_recognition server. receiving message, client sends goal server. registering relevant call functions, client receives feedback result information execution goals server prints information terminal. Launch terminal run roscore separate terminal publish video stream topic /camera/image_raw. usb_cam publish images web cam Install usb_cam package Run rosrun usb_cam usb_cam_node usb_cam_node/image_raw:=camera/image_raw _image_height:=<usb_cam_height> _image_width:=<usb_cam_width> separate terminals run face recognition server client rosrun face_recognition Fserver rosrun face_recognition Fclient terminal publish messages topic /fr_order test face recognition functionalities (notice info printed client terminal command) rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal "your_name" acquire training images face: video stream! rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal retrain update database, recognized rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal recognize faces continuously. stop preempt cancel goal. lets preempt sending goal. rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal "your\_friend's\_name" add training images person rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal recognize rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal recognize continuous rostopic pub /fr_order face_recognition/FRClientGoal exit

description: Plugin for nose or py.test that automatically reruns flaky tests.
readme: flaky Flaky plugin nose py.test automatically reruns flaky tests. Ideally, tests reliably pass fail, test fixtures rely components reliable. flaky, removing tests marking \@skip, automatically retried. information flaky, presentation. Marking tests flaky mark test flaky, simply import flaky decorate test \@flaky: {.sourceCode .python} flaky import flaky {.sourceCode .python} @flaky def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') default, flaky retry failing test behavior overridden passing values flaky decorator. accepts parameters: max_runs, min_passes; flaky run tests max_runs times, succeeded min_passes times. test passes min_passes times, considered success; run max_runs times passing min_passes times, considered failure. {.sourceCode .python} @flaky(max_runs=3, min_passes=2) def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): test pass run times.””” value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double ‘Result doubled incorrectly.’) Marking class flaky addition marking single test flaky, entire test cases marked flaky: {.sourceCode .python} @flaky class TestMultipliers(TestCase): def test_flaky_doubler(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') @flaky(max_runs=3) def test_flaky_tripler(self): value_to_triple result get_result_from_flaky_tripler(value_to_triple) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_triple 'Result tripled incorrectly.') \@flaky class decorator mark test_flaky_doubler flaky, override max_runs test_flaky_tripler decorator test method). rerun types failures Depending tests, failures due flakiness. rerunning failures, filter function flaky fail test {.sourceCode .python} def is_not_crash(err, *args): return issubclass(err[0], ProductCrashedError) @flaky def test_something(): raise ProductCrashedError @flaky(rerun_filter=is_not_crash) def test_something_else(): raise ProductCrashedError Flaky run [test\_something]{.title-ref} run [test\_something\_else]{.title-ref} incur delay test retries: {.sourceCode .python} import time def delay_rerun(*args): time.sleep(1) return True @flaky(rerun_filter=delay_rerun) def test_something_else(): Activating plugin nose plugin, flaky activated command line: {.sourceCode .console} nosetests –with-flaky py.test, flaky automatically run. disabled command line: {.sourceCode .console} py.test no:flaky Command line arguments Flaky Report Pass --no-flaky-report suppress report run detailing flaky test results. Shorter Flaky Report Pass --no-success-flaky-report suppress information successful flaky tests. Force Flaky Pass --force-flaky treat tests flaky. Pass --max-runs=MAX_RUNS and/or --min-passes=MIN_PASSES control behavior flaky --force-flaky Flaky decorators individual tests override defaults. Additional usage examples code test/test_example.py Installation install, simply: {.sourceCode .console} pip install flaky Compatibility Flaky tested test runners options: Nosetests. Doctests marked flaky. Py.test. Works [pytest-xdist]{.title-ref} [\--boxed]{.title-ref} option. Doctests marked flaky. Contributing [CONTRIBUTING.rst](https://github.com/box/flaky/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst). Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands for "generate tests". It promotes generative testing, where a single test can execute over a variety of input.
readme: genty Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands "generate tests". promotes generative testing, single test execute variety input. Genty breeze. file sample.py code test tests: {.sourceCode .python} genty import genty, genty_repeat, genty_dataset unittest import TestCase Here’s class test class MyClass(object): def add_one(self, return Here’s test code @genty class MyClassTests(TestCase): @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) Running `MyClassTests` default unittest runner {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_add_one(0, (sample.MyClassTests) test_add_one(100000, (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests write multiple independent tests test cases, code refactored parametrized genty! produces readable tests. produces maintainable tests. produces expressive tests. option running test multiple times. stress tests exercising code race conditions. test {.sourceCode .python} @genty_repeat(3) def test_adding_one_to_zero(self): self.assertEqual(1, MyClass().add_one(0)) run times, producing output {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_adding_one() iteration_1 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_2 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_3 (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests techniques combined: {.sourceCode .python} @genty_repeat(2) @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) test_complex(default_case) (sample.MyClassTests) test_complex(limit_case) (sample.MyClassTests) test_complex(error_case) (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests `@genty_datasets` chained semantically datasets keeping separate expressiveness. {.sourceCode .python} @genty_dataset(10, @genty_dataset('first', 'second') def test_composing(self, parameter_value): run tests, producing output {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_composing(10) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(100) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(u’first’) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(u’second’) description: Grasp Synergies (aka eigengrasps): compute, store, and command low-dimensional grasp representations.
readme: Grasp Synergy ROS package package implements grasp synergies (aka eigengrasps): low-dimensional representation hand configurations. basically, grasp synergies computed PCA bunch hand configurations (joint angles) infer low-dimensional representation. Points low-dimensional space reprojected full configuration space control hand. ROS package independent hand configuration. consists components: GraspSynergy class: compute store synergies high-dimensional grasp configurations, compute grasps low-dimensional input. synergy_node: ROS magic provide nice interfaces controlling grasp synergies. grasp_synergy Grasp Synergies (aka eigengrasps) low-dimensional representations grasp learned PCA. GraspSynergy class convenience class computing, storing, commanding grasp synergies. hand-agnostic. Grasping data form: numpy matrix joint values list sensor_msgs/JointState messages filepath rosbag file. number collected grasps full dimensionality hand. extract principal components (synergies), practice smaller number. compute grasp original space), simply pass B-dimensional vector coefficients compute_grasp. (Nominally, method automatically figure components return grasp configuration original D-dimensional space. synergy_node synergy node enables load grasp synergy space, create subscribers synergy space, publish desired joint state messages control hand. creates top-level subscriber fully-specified coefficient vector per-component subscribers component single time node receives low-dimensional (synergy-space) point, computes hand configuration publishes message desired joint states. Subscribers: node create variable number subscribers: fully-specified coefficient vector, component singleton values). default, node trained bag file hand configuration data. subscribers synergies: /grasp_synergy [std_msgs/Float32MultiArray] /grasp_synergy/syn_0 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_1 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_2 [std_msgs/Float32] /grasp_synergy/syn_3 [std_msgs/Float32] enables tools rqt_ez_publisher (http://wiki.ros.org/rqt_ez_publisher) provide nice slider GUI interface synergy space: NOTE: Due small bug rqt_ez_publisher, sliders control individual synergies (/grasp_synergy/syn_N) latest rqt_ez_publisher (checkout github workspace run local copy). Publisher: desired joint state topic

map_manager
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Raphael Memmesheimer, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer
readme: map_manager Issues Todo’s Aus bisher ungeklärten Gründen kann seltenen Fällen passieren, dass der map_manager die Verbindung zum roscore verliert. diesem Fall muss durch rosrun map_manager map_manager neugestartet werden. Introduction Der map_manager ist der Mittelpunkt der Kommunikation zwischen homer_mapping, homer_navigation, GUI und die Spiel-Nodes. Das Zusammenspiel dieser Nodes ist Screenshot des rqt_graphs sehen. verwaltet die aktuell durch das mapping erstellte Karte sowie weitere Kartebenen. Aktuell sind das die SLAM-Karte, die aktuellen Laserdaten einer weiteren Ebene und eine Masking-Ebene, der mit Hilfe der GUI Hindernisse oder freie Flächen die Karte gezeichnet werden können. Jedes mal, wenn eine SLAM-Karte von der mapping-Node geschickt wird, wird diese mit allen anderen Karteneben überschrieben der Reihenfolge SLAM, Masking, Laserdaten) und als eine zusammengefügte Karte versendet. Zudem verwaltet der map_manager alle erstellten Points Interest (POIs), die als Ziele die Navigation verwendet werden. Die Node ist außerdem zuständig das Speichern und Laden der Kartenebenen und der POIs. Dabei wird die SLAM-Ebene sowie die Masking-Ebene berücksichtigt. Topics Publisher /map: Die aktuelle Karte, die aus allen aktivierten Kartenebenen zusammengesetzt ist. Diese wird der GUI angezeigt und die Navigation verwendet. /map_manager/poi_list: Verschickt einen Vektor mit allen aktuellen POIs. Dieser Publisher wird immer ausgelöst, sobald sich ein POI ändert oder ein neuer hinzugefügt wird. /map_manager/loaded_map: Wenn eine Karte geladen wird, wird über dieses Topic die geladene SLAM-Ebene die homer_mapping-Node verschickt. /map_manager/mask_slam: Über die GUI kann die SLAM-Map verändert werden. Diese Modifizierungen werden über dieses Topic vom map_manager das homer_mapping versendet. Subscriber /homer_mapping/slam_map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): Hierüber wird die aktuelle SLAM-Map empfangen. /map_manager/save_map (map_messages/SaveMap): Hierüber wird der Befehl zum Speichern der Karte inklusive des Dateinamens empfangen. /map_manager/load_map (map_messages/SaveMap): Hiermit wird eine Karte geladen und alle bisherigen Kartenebenen durch die geladenen ersetzt. /map_manager/toggle_map_visibility (map_messages/MapLayers): Hierüber können einzelne Kartenebenen aktiviert beziehungsweise deaktiviert werden. Deaktivierte werden nicht mehr beim Zusammenfügen der Karte berücksichtigt und dementsprechend auch nicht der GUI angezeigt sowie die Navigation verwendet. /scan (nav_msgs/LaserScan): Der aktuelle Laserscan, der die Laserscan-Ebene gezeichnet wird. /map_manager/add_POI (map_messages/PointOfInterest): Hierüber kann ein POI hinzugefügt werden. /map_manager/modify_POI (map_messages/ModifyPOI): Hierüber kann ein vorhandener POI verändert werden Position,…) /map_manager/delete_POI (map_messages/DeletePointOfInterest): Hierüber kann ein vorhander POI gelöscht werden. /map_manager/modify_map (map_messages/ModifyMap): Über dieses Topic werden die Koordinaten der Polygone verschickt, die über die GUI maskiert wurden. Außerdem wird die Kartenebene mitgeteilt, die verändet werden soll (SLAM oder Masking-Ebene). /map_manager/reset_maps (std_msgs/Empty): Hierüber werden alle Kartenebenen zurückgesetzt.

description: mapnav_msgs
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer
readme: map_messages Introduction Dieses Package enthält alle benutzerdefinierten Messages, die neben den ROS enthaltenen Messages das Mapping und die Navigation verwendet werden. Das Package enthält keine Node oder Librabries. Messages PointOfInterest Die PointOfInterest-Message enthält alle Informationen, einen POI erstellen, verschicken und speichern. PointOfInterest.msg int32 DEFAULT=100 int32 VICTIM=200 int32 OBJECT=300 int32 GRIPPABLE_OBJECT=400 int32 PERSON=600 int32 ROOMBA=700 int32 HAZARD_MATERIAL=800 int32 START_POSITION=900 int32 START_ORIENTATION=1000 int32 type string string remarks geometry_msgs/Pose pose type bezeichnet den Typ des POIs. kann eine der dieser Message vorhandenen Konstanten verwendet werden. bezeichnet den Namen des POIs. Dieser muss einzigartig sein, die POIs über ihren Namen unterschieden werden. remarks: Hier können Anmerkungen reingeschrieben werden. Diese werden der GUI angezeigt. pose bezeichnet die Position und Orientierung des POIS /map-Frame. ModifyPOI ModifyPOI ist dafür zuständig, einen vorhandenen POI verändern. ModifyPOI.msg PointOfInterest poi string old_name poi beinhaltet den POI, durch den der alte ersetzt werden soll. old_name bezeichnet den Namen des POIs, der verändert werden soll. TargetUnreachable TargetUnreachable wird von der Navigation versendet, sobald kein Pfad mehr einem Ziel geplant werden kann. TargetUnreachable.msg int8 UNKNOWN=0 int8 TILT_OCCURED=10 int8 GRAVE_TILT_OCCURED=15 int8 STALL_OCCURED=20 int8 LASER_OBSTACLE=30 int8 reason reason kann einen von den dieser Message definierten Konstanten annehmen und beschreibt den Grund des Fehlers. SaveMap SaveMap wird versendet, wenn eine Karte gespeichert oder geladen werden soll und beinhaltet den Dateipfad zum Kartenordner. SaveMap.msg string filename filename bezeichnet den Dateipfad zum Kartenordner. PointsOfInterest PointsOfInterest wird verwendet, alle aktuellen POIs versenden. PointsOfInterest.msg PointOfInterest[] pois pois beinhaltet einen Vektor mit allen aktuellen POIs. StartNavigation StartNavigation ist eine von zwei Methoden, eine Navigation starten. Hier wird Der POI mitgegeben, dem der Roboter navigieren soll. StartNavigation.msg geometry_msgs/Pose goal float32 distance_to_target bool skip_final_turn bool fast_planning goal beinhaltet den Ziel-POI distance_to_target: Hier kann angegeben werden, welcher Distanz zum Ziel die Navigation als erfolgreich abgeschlossen wird. skip_final_turn: Hier kann eingestellt werden, der Roboter sich Ziel-POI Richtund der POI-Orientierung ausrichten soll oder nicht. fast_planning: Mit dieser Option kann ein experimentelles “Schnelles Planen” eingeschaltet werden. werden nur Pfade geplant, die sich einer Boundingbox zwischen Roboter und Zielposition befinden. MapLayers MapLayers definiert die vorhanden Kartenebenen als Konstanten und kann zudem verwendet werden, einzelne Ebenen ein- oder auszuschalten. MapLayers.msg int32 SLAM_LAYER=0 int32 MASKING_LAYER=1 int32 KINECT_LAYER=2 int32 SICK_LAYER=3 int32 layer bool state layer enthält die Kartenebenen-ID und kann einen Wert dern dieser Message definierten Konstanten annehmen. state besagt, die ausgewählte Kartenebene aktiviert sein soll. NavigateToPOI NavigateToPOI ist die zweite Art eine Navigation starten. Anstatt das gesamte POI-Objekt mitzugeben, wird nur der eines bereits map_manger vorhandenen POIs mitgegeben, der daraufhin von der Navigation nachgeschlagen wird. NavigateToPOI.msg string poi_name float32 distance_to_target bool skip_final_turn poi_name beschreibt den Namen des POIS, dem navigiert werden soll. distance_to_target siehe StartNavigation skip_final_turn siehe StartNavigation ModifyMap Mit dieser Message können Bereiche einzelnen Kartenebenen verändert werden. ModifyMap.msg int32 FREE int32 BLOCKED int32 OBSTACLE int32 NOT_MASKED geometry_msgs/Point[] region int32 maskAction int32 mapLayer region beschreibt die Eckpunkte des Polygons, das maskiert werden soll. maskAction kann einen Wert der dieser Message definierten Konstanten annehmen. OBSTACLE wird der Karte rot dargestellt. Mit NOT_MASKED können bereits maskierte Bereiche wieder gelöscht werden. mapLayer enthält die der verändernden Kartenebene. Die IDs sind der Message MapLayers definiert. DeletePointOfInterest Löscht einen vorhandenen POI. DeletePointOfInterest.msg string beschreibt den Namen des löschenden POIs. DoMapping Mit dieser Message kann das Mapping ein- oder ausgeschaltet werden. DoMapping.msg bool state state beinhaltet den Zustand des Mappings (true false Services GetPointsOfInterest Über diesen Service kann die aktuelle Liste der POIs angefordert werden. GetPointsOfInterest.srv PointsOfInterest poi_list poi_list beinhaltet einen Vektor mit allen aktuellen POIs.

description: homer_mapping
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Raphael Memmesheimer, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer
readme: homer_mapping Introduction Das Package homer_mapping besteht aus einer gleichnamigen Node. Diese ist die Lokalisierung und Kartierung des Roboters mit Hilfe der Odometrie des Roboters und eines Laserscanners zuständig. Das SLAM-Problem wird durch den Partikelfilter-Algorithmus gelöst. Die Node erwartet kontiniuierlich Odometrie-Werte und Laserdaten und verschickt konstanten Abständen korrigierte Poseschätzungen über das Topic /pose und tf-Transformation /map /base_link. Zudem kann der Roboter sich auf einer vorher geladenen Karte lokalisieren, sowie eine aktuell erstellte Karte abgespeichert werden. besteht die Option, die Kartierung ein- oder auszuschalten. Beim Laden einer Karte wird die Kartierung automatisch ausgeschaltet. Topics Publisher /pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped): Die aktuell ermittelte Pose relativ Karte Frame /map) des Roboters aus dem Partikelfilter. /homer_mapping/slam_map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): Das aktuelle Karte des Roboters. Subscriber /odom (nav_msgs/Odometry): Die aktuellen Odometrie-Werte vom Roboter. Diese werden die Partikelfilter benötigt. /scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan): Die aktuelle Lasermessung, die vom Partikelfilter benötigt wird. /homer_mapping/userdef_pose (geometry_msgs/Pose): Mit diesem Topic kann die die aktuell vom Partikelfilter berechnete Pose auf eine benutzerdefinierte gesetzt werden. Der Partikelfilter arbeitet nun mit dieser weiter. /homer_mapping/do_mapping (map_messages/DoMapping): Mit diesem Topic kann die Kartierung ein- oder ausgeschaltet werden. /map_manager/reset_maps (std_msgs/Empty): Hiermit kann die aktuelle Karte zurückgesetzt werden. /map_manager/loaded_map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): Mit diesem Topic kann die aktuelle Karte durch eine andere (geladene) Karte ausgetauscht werden. /map_manager/mask_slam (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): OccupancyGrid dieses Topics stehen Informationen, welche Teile der aktuellen Karte durch andere Werte (frei oder belegt) ersetzt werden sollen. Launch Files homer_mapping.launch: Dieses Launchfile lädt die Parameterdatei homer_mapping.yaml und startet die Node homer_mapping sowie die Node map_manager gleichnamigen Package. homer_mapping_rviz.launch:`Dieses Launchfile hat die gleiche Funktionalität wie das obige, wobei zusätzlich rviz startet. Parameter homer_mapping.yaml /homer_mapping/size: Size beschreibt die Größe einer Seite der Karte Metern. Die Karte ist quadratisch /homer_mapping/resolution: Resolution ist die Länge einer (quadratischen) Zelle der Karte Metern /homer_mapping/backside_checking: Wenn auf “true” gesetzt, wird verhindet, dass Vorder- und Rückseite einer dickeren Wand auf die gematcht werden. /homer_mapping/obstacle_borders: Wenn auf “true” gesetzt, wird eingetragene Hindernisse ein kleiner Rand unbekanntes Gebiet gelassen. /homer_mapping/measure_sampling_step: Minimale Distanz Metern, die zwischen zwei aufeinanderfolgenden Messpunkten Laserscan vorhanden sein muss, sie die Poseberechnung verwenden /homer_mapping/laser_scanner/free_reading_distance: Minimale Distanz Metern, die als hindernissfrei angenommen wird, wenn der aktuelle Messpunkt fehlerhaft ist /particlefilter/error_values/rotation_error_rotating: Rotationsfehler Prozent, der beim Rotieren des Roboters auftritt /particlefilter/error_values/rotation_error_translating: Rotationsfehler Grad, der beim Fahren von einem Meter auftritt /particlefilter/error_values/translation_error_translating: Translationsfehler Prozent, der beim Geradeausfahren auftritt /particlefilter/error_values/translation_error_rotating: Translationsfehler Metern, der beim Rotieren von einem Grad auftritt /particlefilter/error_values/move_jitter_while_turning: Streuung der neu berechneten Pose Meter pro Grad Drehung /particlefilter/hyper_slamfilter/particlefilter_num: Anzahl der Partikelfilter Hyperpartikelfilter (ist standardmäßig auf gesetzt) /particlefilter/particle_num: Anzahl der Partikel jedem Partikelfilter /particlefilter/max_rotation_per_second: Maximale Rotation Radiant pro Sekunde, die der Roboter sich drehen darf, ohne dass das Mapping ausgesetzt wird /particlefilter/wait_time: Minimale Zeit, die zwischen zwei Mapping-Schritten verstrichen sein muss /particlefilter/update_min_move_angle: Minimale Rotation Grad, die durchgeführt werden muss, damit ein Partikelfilterschritt ausgeführt wird… /particlefilter/update_min_move_dist: …oder minimale Distanz Metern, die der Roboter geradeaus fährt… /particlefilter/max_update_interval: …oder minimale Wartezeit, der der Roboter steht. /selflocalization/scatter_var_xy: Streuung der Partikel x/y-Richtung Metern beim Setzen einer benutzerdefinierten Pose /selflocalization/scatter_var_theta: Streuung der Ausrichtung der Partikel Radiant beim Setzen einer benutzerdefinierten Pose

description: The nav_libs package
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer, Raphael Memmesheimer
readme: nav_libs Issues Todo’s Package nav_libs ist eine Kopie der Robbie-Bibliothek Math, umbenannt MappingMath, die nur von homer_mapping benutzt werden soll, solange keine vernünftige Alternative die Pose gibt. Introduction Das Package nav_libs enthält einige Bibliotheken, die vom Package homer_mapping und nav_libs verwendet werden. Außerdem enthält Ordner tools die Header-Datei tools.h, die Funktionen zum Transformieren verschiedene Koordinatenframes enthält. diese Funktionen befinden sich namespace “map_tools”. Die Bibliothek Explorer wird von homer_mapping und homer_navigation verwendet und enthält die Pfadplanungsalgorithmen A-Stern sowie die dafür benötigte Datenstruktur der GridMap. Die Bibliothek SpeedControl wird von homer_navigation verwendet und ist dafür zuständig abhängig von den aktuellen Laserdaten die höchstzulässige Geschwindigkeit berechnen. Die Bibliothek MappingMath wird von homer_mapping verwendet und enthält die Datenstruktur Pose, der die aktuelle Roboterposition innerhalb der Node gespeichert wird.

description: The homer_navigation package
maintainers: Viktor Seib, Niklas Yann Wettengel, Gregor Heuer, Raphael Memmesheimer
readme: homer_navigation Issues Todo’s Die Pfadplanung dauert Gegensatz zur alten Robbie-Implementierung lange. mehr freie Fläche vorhanden ist, desto länger dauert die Berechnung eines Pfades. Introduction Das Package “homer_navigation” mit seiner gleichnamigen Node ist die Navigation des Roboters zuständig. hält stets die aktuelle Karte des Roboters vor, die vom map_manager veschickt wurde und berechnet anhand von dieser Karte einen Pfad vom Roboter einem gegebenen Zielpunkt. Dafür verwendet die Navigation die sogenannte Pfad-Transformation eine Kombination aus Distanz-Transformation und Hindernistransformation Verbindung mit A-Stern, einen optimalen Pfad finden. Das Verhältnis zwischen Distanz- und Hindernis-Transformation kann über einen Parameter angepasst werden. Nach der Pfadberechnung ist das Package außerdem dafür zuständig den Roboter diesen Pfad zum Ziel abfahren lassen. Topics Publisher /robot_platform/MoveRobot (robot_platform/MoveRobot): Über dieses Topic wird der aktuelle Fahrbefehl die Roboterplattform geschickt. /robot_platform/TurnRobot (robot_platform/TurnRobot): Über dieses Topic wird der aktuelle Drehbefehl die Roboterplattform geschickt. /robot_platform/StopRobot (robot_platform/StopRobot): Über dieses Topic wird der Roboter Ziel beziehungsweise vor einem Hindernis gestoppt. /homer_navigation/target_reached (std_msgs/Empty): Wenn der Roboter sein Ziel erreicht hat, wird eine Message über dieses Topic veschickt. /homer_navigation/target_unreachable (map_messages/TargetUnreachable): Über dieses Topic wird mitgeteilt, wenn der Roboter sein Ziel nicht erreichen kann und die Navigation abgebrochen wurde. Ein Statuscode wird mitgeliefert (siehe Package map_messages). /homer_navigation/path (nav_msgs/Path): Hierüber wird der aktuelle Pfad zur Visualisierung die GUI geschickt. Subscriber /map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid): Die jeweils aktuelle Map wird empfangen, sie die Pfadplanung und Hindernisvermeidung während der Navigation verwenden. /pose (geometry_msgs/PoseStamped): Die aktuelle Pose des Roboters wird als Startpunkt der Pfadplanung verwendet. /scan (nav_msgs/LaserScan): Der aktuelle LaserScan wird zur Hindernisvermeidung verwendet. /robot_platform/task_finished (std_msgs/Empty): Wird vom Roboter nach erfolgter AUsführung einer Bewegung verschickt. Die Navigation verwendet dies Ende der Navigation, nach der letzten Drehung auf dem Zielpunkt die TargetReached-Nachricht versenden. /homer_navigation/start_navigation (map_messages/StartNavigation): Startet die Pfadplanung und anschließend die Navigation zur mitgelieferten Zielpose /homer_navigation/stop_navigation (map_messages/StopNavigation): Stoppt die aktuelle Navigation. /homer_navigation/navigate_to_POI (map_messages/NavigateToPOI): Startet die Pfadplanung und anschließend die Navigation zum POI mit angegebenen Namen. Der entsprechende POI wird anschließend vom map_manager erfragt. /homer_navigation/unknown_threshold (std_msgs/Int8): Über dieses Topic kann der Schwellwert verändert werden, dem ein Zellwahrscheinlichkeit als belegt und damit unbefahrbar markiert wird. Standard ist Launch Files homer_navigation.launch: Startet die Navigation und lädt alle Navigations-Paramter den Parameterserver. Parameter homer_navigation /homer_navigation/safe_path_weight: Gewicht, Bevorzugung sicherer größerer Abstand Hindernissen) Pfade Gegensatz kürzeren Pfaden einzustellen. bedeutet kürzester Pfad, aber keine Sicherheit. höher, desto sicherer. /homer_navigation/allowed_obstacle_distance/min: Mindestdistanz zum nächsten Hindernis, die der Roboter bei der Navigation besitzen muss. /homer_navigation/allowed_obstacle_distance/max: Maximaldistanz zum nächsten Hindernis, die der Roboter bei der Navigation besitzen muss (wichtig die Lokalisierung, abhängig von der Reichweite der Abstandssensoren). /homer_navigation/safe_obstacle_distance/min: Mindestdistanz des Roboters zum nächsten Hindernis eine sichere Navigation. Gewichtung wird mit safe_path_weight eingestellt. /homer_navigation/safe_obstacle_distance/max: Maximaldistanz des Roboters zum nächsten Hindernis. /homer_navigation/frontier_safeness_factor: Faktor der minimal erlaubten Distanz zum nächsten Hindernis, den eine Grenzzelle unbekanntem Gebiet mindestens haben muss, als sicher und damit befahrbar gelten. /homer_navigation/collision_distance: Wenn die Distanz zum nächsten Hindernis Metern (gemessen der Front des Roboters) kleiner wird als der angegebene Parameter, wird der aktuelle Fahrbefehl gestoppt und die Pfadplanung neugestartet. /homer_navigation/collision_distance_near_target: Nähe Metern zum Ziel, der der Roboter bei einer Kollision nicht mehr zurückfährt und neuplant, sondern stoppt und eine TargetReached-Nachricht versendet. /homer_navigation/backward_distance: Strecke Metern, die der Roboter bei einer bevorstehenden Kollision zurückfährt, bevor einen neuen Pfad plant. /homer_navigation/check_path: Wenn auf “true” gesetzt, wird der aktuell geplante Pfad auf neu hinzukommende Hindernisse geprüft und gegebenenfalls neugeplant. /homer_navigation/check_path_max_errors: Wenn Pfade gecheckt werden, wird umgeplant, sobald sich “check_path_max_errors”-mal hintereinander ein Hindernis der ankommenden Karte Pfad befindet. /homer_navigation/check_path_max_distance: Der Pfad wird nur der Nähe des Roboters bis zur Parameter angegebenen Distanz auf neu hinzukommende Hindernisse überprüft /homer_navigation/turn_threshold_angle: Wenn der Winkel zwischen der Orientierung des Roboters und der Strecke zum nächsten Wegpunkt unter dem angegebenen Wert liegt, soll geradeaus gefahren werden, ansonsten soll sich der Roboter diesen Winkel Richtung des Wegpunkts drehen. /homer_navigation/waypoint_sampling_threshold: Parameter, Anzahl der Wegpunkte Pfad minimieren. bedeutet keine Reduzierung. höher der Wert, desto mehr Wegpunkte werden gelöscht. /homer_navigation/speed_control/last_speedfactor_count: Größe des Vektors, der die zuletzt berechneten Geschwindigkeitsfaktoren berechnet. Wird benutzt, aus dem Mittelwert zusammen mit der aktuellen Distanz zum nächsten Hindernis die aktuelle Geschwindigkeit berechnen. /homer_navigation/speed_control/min_move_speedfactor: Minimaler Faktor der maximal möglichen Geschwindigkeit, mit der der Roboter jemals fahren darf. /homer_navigation/speed_control/min_turn_speedfactor_moving: Minimaler Faktor der maximal möglichen Drehgeschwindigkeit, mit der der Roboter sich während einer Geradeausfahrt drehen darf (aktuell mit dem cu2wd nicht möglich). /homer_navigation/speed_control/min_turn_speedfactor_standing: Minimaler Faktor der maximal möglichen Drehgeschwindigkeit, mit der sich der Roboter Stand drehen darf. /homer_navigation/speed_control/inner_danger_zone/x_min: Wenn sich der mit den nächsten vier Parametern beschriebenen Boundingbox den Roboter ein Hindernis befindet, darf sich der Roboter nur mit der vorher eingestellten nachfolgend eingestellten Geschwindigkeit bewegen. /homer_navigation/speed_control/inner_danger_zone/x_max: /homer_navigation/speed_control/inner_danger_zone/y_min: /homer_navigation/speed_control/inner_danger_zone/y_max: /homer_navigation/speed_control/inner_danger_zone/speed_factor: Faktor der maximalen Geschwindigkeit, mit der sich der Roboter seiner inneren Gefahrenzone bewegen darf. /homer_navigation/speed_control/outer_danger_zone/x_min: Eine zweite Boundingbox, die die innere umschließen muss. /homer_navigation/speed_control/outer_danger_zone/x_max: /homer_navigation/speed_control/outer_danger_zone/y_min: /homer_navigation/speed_control/outer_danger_zone/y_max: /homer_navigation/speed_control/outer_danger_zone/speed_factor: Faktor der maximalen Geschwindigkeit, mit der sich der Roboter seiner äußeren Gefahrenzone bewegen darf. /homer_navigation/max_rot_vel: Maximal mögliche Rotationsgeschwindigkeit des Roboters Radiants/Sekunde. /homer_navigation/max_trans_vel: Maximal mögliche Geschwindigkeit des Roboters Metern/Sekunde.

    so these message types are largely compatible.
    This message package was creates to enable a MoveIt-compatible, but
    not MoveIt!-dependable interface for other ROS packages dealing with
    object information.

readme: kobuki_led_controller Kobuki Led Blinker Usage import kobuki_led_controller rospy.init_node('Hello') blinker kobuki_led_controller.LedBlinker() rospy.loginfo("Blinker blinker start") blinker.start() Starts Led blinker thread. rospy.loginfo("Blinker blinks red") blinker.set_on_error() Blinks Red LED rospy.sleep(3.0) rospy.loginfo("Blinker blinks Green") blinker.set_on_ok() blinks Green LED rospy.sleep(3.0) rospy.loginfo("Blinker stop blinking") blinker.set_on_off() stop blinking rospy.loginfo("Blinker blinker stops") blinker.stop() stops led blinkder thread.

description:
The lama_interfaces package provides the interfaces between ROS nodes
	  (such as jockeys from lama_jockeys) and the map.

The lama_jockeys package contains base classes for learning,
  localizing, and navigating jockeys.
Framework for robot autonomy in Large Maps (LaMa), core functionalities
authors: Gaël Ecorchard, Karel Košnar, Vojtěch Vonásek

description:
lama_msgs provides messages for the lama framework such as
	  Frontier, Crossing.
The fake_laser package

Implements a localizing jockey for the
	  Large Maps framework (LaMa) based on a local costmap (costmap position is
	  relative to the sensor but orientation is absolute).

	  Implement a localizing jockey from a LaserScan. The associated
	  descriptors are LaserScan[] and Crossing.

The nj_costmap package implements a navigation jockey for the
	  Large Maps framework (LaMa) based on a local costmap (costmap position is
	  relative to the sensor but orientation is absolute).

The nj_oa_costmap package is a navigating jockey for the Large
	  Maps framework. Its role is to drive the robot forward while avoiding
	  obstacles. It uses a local map which position is relative to the robot
	  but which orientation is constant.

The anj_featurenav package provides a learning jockey and a
    navigating jockey for the Large Maps framework (LaMa). It learns a
    path by saving image features and is able to follow the same path.
    It is based on algorithms provided by OpenCV (free ones).
authors: Gaël Ecorchard, Karel Košnar, Vladimír Petrík

description:
The featurenav_base package provides base functionality
    for learning and navigating jockeys based on feature detection for
    the Large Maps Framework (LaMa).

Localizing jockey from LaserScan

	  Implement a localizing jockey from a LaserScan. The associated descriptor
	  are LaserScan[] and Crossing.

Localizing jockey from LaserScan and absolute heading.
    Implements a localizing jockey from a LaserScan and absolute heading.
    The associated descriptors are sensor_msgs/LaserScan[] and
    lama_msgs/Crossing.
The nj_laser package
authors: Gaël Ecorchard, Karel Košnar, Vojtěch Vonásek

description:
The nj_oa_laser package is a navigating jockey for the Large
	  Maps framework. Its role is to drive the robot forward while avoiding
	  obstacles. It uses a LaserScan.

description:
The local_map package
	  The local_map package takes as input a LaserScan message and outputs
	  a local map as OccupancyGrid. The local map orientation is the same
	  as the one of the global frame.

The map_ray_caster package provides a class for cached ray casting
  on maps. The origin of all rays is fixed relative to the map, it is the map center.
The nj_escape_crossing package is a navigating jockey used to move a robot from away from a crossing

    It contains the base class from which laser scan matcher wrappers
    should inherit from.

readme: lower_step_detector Summary 3号機の斜め下向きLRFで下方段差を検出する機能 Directory third_robot_lower_step_detector config yaml config files launch launch files scripts python codes Preparation /config/lower_step_detector.yaml laser_intensity_max: margine_between_plane_and_down_step: 計算上平面と思うintensityから何[m]以上大きい場合に段差とみなすか．デフォルトは1.0[m] virtual_laser_intensity: laser_scan_range_deg: LRF detect_step_angle_min_deg: LRF chunk_angle_for_noise_deg: ratio_detect_in_chunk: ノイズ対策．セグメント内でどの程度閾値を超えたら段差と判定するか．0.8[raito]. launch roslaunch third_robot_lower_step_detector lower_step_detector_demo.launch roslaunch third_robot_lower_step_detector lower_step_detector_demo.launch sub_topic:=hogehoge これで、検出プログラムとrviz が起動します。この後bagファイル再生等をして下さい。 デフォルトでは、/base_scan1をpublish すれば、修正後の情報である/base_scan1_fixがpublish トピックの変更をした場合、このノードがsubscribeするtopic名を/base_scan1から/hogehogeに変えられます。publish されるトピックは/hogehoge_fix

description: This is a ROS package used for the mult-task allocation in a robot team. It is based on Multi-Agent theory and is an abstraction of ALLIANCE model. A cooperative robot team is a multi-agent robot system in essence. In the team, each robot can be seen as an intelligent agent. We have developed a prototype system by python hitherto. The nodelet-based C++ version is being developed and will be released in short time periods.
authors: Minglong Li, Xiaodong Yi, Yanzhen Wang, Zhongxuan Cai

description:
A moveit_controller_manager implementation which supports execution
    of a MultiDOF-trajectory with a virtual joint.
    Transforms the moveit_msgs::RobotTrajectory into a path navigation action
    and/or a control_msgs/FollowJointTrajectoryAction
    to control (1) the virtual joint with the path navigation action (read from
    moveit_msgs/RobotTrajectory.multi_dof_joint_trajectory), and (2) the
    joints with a control_msgs/FollowJointTrajectoryAction.

file: #!/usr/bin/env python import logging import nasa_common_logging import import rospkg ncl_path rospkg.RosPack().get_path('nasa_common_logging') config_file os.path.join(ncl_path, 'share', description: Rviz 3-D visualizer for planning algorithms implemented with the Open Motion Planning Library (OMPL)
readme: OMPL Visual Tools OMPL Visual Tools library visualizing debugging Open Motion Planning Library algorithms Rviz. Formally named ompl_rviz_viewer August project spun internship Willow Garage. Video ompl_visual_tools originally develped testing cost-based algorithms dimensional space dimension displayed cost. space grey scale cost map image passed program. lighter (closer white) pixel image “higher cost” considered Black considered cost. Additionally, absolute obstacles defining max limit cost, threshold considered obstacle. program similar OMPL.app distributed OMPL, RViz visualization streamlined costs experience-based planning. Developed Dave Coleman University Colorado Boulder Willow Garage Status: Travis Devel Job Status AMD64 Debian Job Status Build sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-ompl-visual-tools Usage library integrated project easily view robot planning environment Rviz. load visualizer: visual tools interfacing Rviz ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; Load tool displaying Rviz visual_tools_.reset(new ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualTools(BASE_FRAME)); visual_tools_->setSpaceInformation(si_); visual_tools_->setGlobalScale(100); Clear current rviz makers visual_tools_->deleteAllMarkers(); Dimensions optional cost map test environment cost map: Cost ompl::base::CostMap2DOptimizationObjectivePtr cost_map_; cost_map_->max_cost_threshold_percent_ max_cost_threshold_percent; cost_map_->loadImage(image_path); Pass cost visualizer visual_tools_->setCostMap(cost_map_->cost_); view cost map Rviz: visual_tools_->publishCostMap(cost_map_->image_); view start goal location: visual_tools_->publishState(start, rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); visual_tools_->publishState(goal, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); view solution path: Interpolate solution simple_setup_->getSolutionPath().interpolate(); Show path visual_tools_->publishPath( simple_setup_->getSolutionPath(), rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, "final_solution"); planner Visualize explored space visual_tools_->publishGraph(planner_data, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, "tree"); Visualize sample locations visual_tools_->publishSamples(planner_data); MoveIt! Robot Planning moveit_visual_tools information tools class MoveIt!. OMPL-specific features: set state space MoveIt! chosen robot OMPL: Create state space describing robot's planning group ompl_interface::ModelBasedStateSpaceSpecification model_ss_spec(moveit_robot_model, joint_model_group); const ompl_interface::JointModelStateSpaceFactory factory; ompl_interface::ModelBasedStateSpacePtr model_state_space factory.getNewStateSpace(model_ss_spec); Setup state space model_state_space->setup(); visual_tools_->setStateSpace(model_state_space); publish paths tips robot, planned OMPL: std::vector<ompl::base::PlannerDataPtr> paths; simple_setup.getAllPlannerDatas(paths); tip links setup std::vector<const robot_model::LinkModel*> tips; joint_model_group_->getEndEffectorTips(tips); bool show_trajectory_animated true; visual_tools_->publishRobotPath(paths[0], joint_model_group, tips, show_trajectory_animated); View Rviz Start Rviz included launch file: roslaunch ompl_visual_tools ompl_rviz.launch Demo planner standard RRT: rosrun ompl_visual_tools rrt_demo optional parameters, pass argument. options. Note: change algorithm manually edit code Cost Map Usage default cost map image located resources/ folder, running algorithm. Optionally pass cost map image command line argument, shown rosrun ompl_visual_tools rrt_demo cost_map.ppm image PPM “Netpbm color image” format. convert jpg, png image format Linux simply “convert” command, shown sudo apt-get install convert convert cost_map.png cost_map.ppm general, pixel image decent space size, larger dimensions require computational resources hang computer. GIMP good editor scaling images TODO Remove PPM library depend OMPL’s version Develop encouraged fork package GitHub test cost-based planning algorithms visualizer!

description: pcan topics is based on transmi- and receivetest. It consists of two nodes; pcan_transmit and pcan_receive. The pcan_receive publishes incomming CAN messages on the pcan_receive topic in string format.

description: Another ISO 8601 parser for Python
readme: aniso8601 ISO parser Python Features Pure Python implementation Python support extra dependencies Logical behavior Parse time, datetime.time Parse date, datetime.date Parse datetime, datetime.datetime Parse duration, datetime.timedelta Parse interval, tuple dates datetimes Parse repeating interval, date datetime generator UTC offset represented fixed-offset tzinfo regular expressions Installation recommended installation method pip: pip install aniso8601 Alternatively, download source (git repository hosted Bitbucket) install python setup.py install Parsing datetimes parse typical ISO datetime string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_datetime('1977-06-10T12:00:00Z') datetime.datetime(1977, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbd90>) Alternative delimiters rl_agent is a package containing reinforcement learning (RL) agents.

rl_common is a package of common RL files needed by both the agent and the
environment. Mainly the interfaces defining their class methods, and some other
utility methods.
rl_env is is a package containing reinforcement learning (RL) environments.
rl_experiment is a package to run RL experiments using the rl_agent and rl_env packages.
rl_msgs is a package of ROS message definitions, which are used for a reinforcement learning agent to communicate with an environment.

readme: remote_monitor Remote monitoring package navigation Demo Video roslaunch cirkit_unit03_navigation_gazebo autorun.launch roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_server.launch gazebo:=true map_yaml:=playpen_map.yaml roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_client.launch Nodes monitor_server 遠隔監視PC側node．serviceで受信した位置と姿勢を地図上に表示させる monitor_client 移動ロボット側node．一定距離毎に現在位置と姿勢をserviceで送信する． デフォルトでは/amcl_poseをsubscribeする． Installation なお<catkin_ws>は，任意のcatkinizeされたワークスペースへのパスを表します． <catkin_ws>/src git clone https://github.com/CIR-KIT/remote_monitor.git <catkin_ws> wstool init src wstool merge src src/remote_monitor/remote_monitor.rosinstall wstool update src rosdep update rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src catkin_make source devel/setup.bash launch service server $(find cirkit_unit03_maps)/map/` roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_server.launch map_yaml:=hogehoge.yaml Gazeboの場合のコマンド roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_server_gazebo.launch map_yaml:=hogehoge.yaml service client /etc/openvpn emacs client.conf remote 101.102.103.xxx OpenVPNを再起動する. service openvpn restart ifconfig 下記のようなtunデバイスが表示される．少し時間がかかる場合がある． tun0 Link encap:不明なネット inetアドレス:10.8.0.6 P-t-P:10.8.0.5 POINTOPOINT RUNNING NOARP MULTICAST MTU:1500 衝突(Collisions):0 そしてサーバにping ping ROSネットワークの設定 export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://10.8.0.6:11311 export ROS_HOST_NAME=10.8.0.6 export ROS_IP=10.8.0.6 下記をsourceでもよい． roscd remote_monitor/scripts source vpn_setting_for_robot.sh env grep ROS ROSネットワークの確認 rosservice list /remote_monitor_robot_pose /remote_monitor_human_pose roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_client.launch Gazebo設定 roslaunch remote_monitor remote_monitor_client_gazebo.launch parameters /config/remote_monitor_server.yaml ratio: /config/third_robot_client.yaml interval_dist: pose_topic: amcl_pose subscribeするトピック名 command current history reset history history ratio 地図を拡大します。historyモードの履歴は削除します。 ratio 地図を縮小します。historyモードの履歴は削除します。

description:
rgbdslam (v2) is a SLAM solution for RGB-D cameras. It provides the current pose of the camera and allows to  
    create a registered point cloud or an octomap. 
    
    It features a GUI interface for easy usage, but can also be controlled by ROS service calls, e.g., when running on a robot.

    For installation and usage instructions see the README file of
authors: Felix Endres, Juergen Hess, Nikolas Engelhard
readme: RGBDSLAMv2 state-of-the-art SLAM system RGB-D cameras, Microsoft Kinect Asus Xtion Pro Live. create point clouds OctoMaps. RGBDSLAMv2 based open source projects, ROS, OpenCV, OpenGL, PCL, OctoMap, SiftGPU, journal article system description performance evaluation publication: Mapping RGB-D Camera”, Endres, Hess, Sturm, Cremers, Burgard, IEEE Transactions Robotics, Additional information www.informatik.uni-freiburg.de/~endres http://www.ros.org/wiki/rgbdslam http://answers.ros.org/questions/tags:rgbdslam Prerequisites Ubuntu ROS indigo Installation Put RGBDSLAMv2 catkin workspace: catkin tutorial details. git clone repository workspace’s “src/” directory. download RGBDSLAMv2 archive extract “src/”. rosdep “rosdep install rgbdslam”) install missing dependencies. details http://wiki.ros.org/ROS/Tutorials/rosdep build RGBDSLAMv2 catkin workspace execute “catkin_make”. error missing siftgpu library, execute “catkin_make” ##Installation Scratch walkthrough tested, watch problems. Assuming installed ROS indigo Ubuntu issue commands terminal (copy-paste work) #Prepare Workspace source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash mkdir ~/rgbdslam_catkin_ws/src ~/rgbdslam_catkin_ws/src catkin_init_workspace ~/rgbdslam_catkin_ws/ catkin_make source devel/setup.bash RGBDSLAM ~/rgbdslam_catkin_ws/src wget http://github.com/felixendres/rgbdslam_v2/archive/indigo.zip unzip indigo.zip ~/rgbdslam_catkin_ws/ #Install rosdep update rosdep install rgbdslam catkin_make Installation What’s sections details usage. started quickly here’s important pointers: RGBDSLAMv2 RGB-D camera install openni (sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-openni-launch). Check launch files “launch/” examples specific cases. roslaunch rgbdslam openni+rgbdslam.launch good starting point live mapping. Check README “test/” running, testing evaluating RGBDSLAMv2 Juergen Sturm’s RGB-D SLAM Dataset Benchmark: http://vision.in.tum.de/data/datasets/rgbd-dataset cython evaluation scripts (sudo apt-get install cython). SURF SIFT, build OpenCV source, including non-free module include SIFTGPU, included, enabled CMakeLists.txt). CMakeLists.txt RGBDSLAMv2 set build directory OpenCV enable non-free functionality. Note SIFT SURF choice. Due (software) features RGBDSLAMv2, ORB outperforms IMPORTANT NOTE software update ROS Fuerte version RGBDSLAM. changed, DOCUMENTATION OUTDATED. report problems documentation. Configuration launch-files set parameters RGB-D SLAM cases. definitive list settings default settings readable definition src/parameter_server.cpp current settings default) GUI Menu Settings->View Current Settings. use-case launch-files work correctly regularly tested. running fiddle topics (“rostopic list” “rosnode info” “rqt_graph” great Usage people registered point cloud. default /rgbdslam/batch_clouds command RGB-D SLAM clouds transformation default /map /openni_camera octoMap library compiled rgbdslam node. create octomap GUI selecting “Save Octomap” “Data” Menu. Online octomapping recommended. Usage GUI start RGBDSLAMv2 launch, roslaunch rgbdslam openni+rgbdslam.launch Alternatively start openni nodes RGBDSLAMv2 separately, roslaunch openni_camera openni_node.launch roslaunch rgbdslam rgbdslam.launch capture models press space start recording continuous stream press enter record single frame. reduce data redundancy, sequential frames position included final model. Parameters RGBDSLAMv2 customizable parameters. set launch file. Parameters changed operation GUI, GUI effect parameters. Visualization visualization shows globally optimized model click update view optimization). Neighbouring points triangulated missing values depth jumps. shortcut triangulation toggled. raw points render slightly faster parameter “cloud_display_type” controls triangulation computed time cloud received. parameter “gl_point_size” users. Usage GUI RosUI alternative Grapical_UI run rgbdslam headless, rgbdslam service-calls. calls /rgbdslam/ros_ui {reset, quick_save, send_all, delete_frame, optimize, reload_config, save_trajectory} /rgbdslam/ros_ui_b {pause, record} {true, false} /rgbdslam/ros_ui_f {set_max} {float} /rgbdslam/ros_ui_s {save_octomap, save_cloud, save_g2o_graph, save_trajectory, save_features, save_individual} {filename} start rgbdslam headless headless.launch: roslaunch rgbdslam headless.launch Capture single frames rosservice call /rgbdslam/ros_ui frame Capture stream data: rosservice call /rgbdslam/ros_ui_b pause false Send point clouds computed transformations rviz octomap_server): rosservice call /rgbdslam/ros_ui send_all Save data pointclouds file quicksave.pcd rgbdslam/bin-directory: rosservice call /rgbdslam/ros_ui_s save_cloud pointcloud file rgbdslam/bin-directory: rosservice call /rgbdslam/ros_ui save_individual /rgbdslam/ros_ui: reset ‘‘resets graph, delets nodes (refreshes capturing images)’’ frame ‘‘capture frame sensor’’ optimize ‘‘trigger graph optimizer’’ reload_config ‘‘reloads paramters ROS paramter server’’ quick_save ‘‘saves pointclouds file quicksave.pcd rgbdslam/bin-directory’’ send_all ‘‘sends pointclouds /rgbdslam/transformed_cloud visualized rviz)’’ delete_frame ‘‘delete frame graph (refreshes capturing images)’’ /rgbdslam/ros_ui_b: pause ''pauses resumes capturing images'' record ''pauses stops recording bag-files, rgbdslam/bin-directory'' /rgbdslam/ros_ui_f: set_max ''filters datapoints saving files)'' /rgbdslam/ros_ui_s: save_features ''saves feature locations descriptors yaml file filename'' save_cloud ''saves cloud filename .ply .pcd)'' save_individual ''saves scan file (appending suffix prefix)'' save_octomap ''saves cloud filename'' save_trajectory ''saves sensor trajectory file _estimate.txt'' compilation lot memory, environment variable $ROS_PARALLEL_JOBS set. located Germany description: The rosshell package
readme:
rosshell simple nodes mediators shell commands. Author: André Dietrich Sebastian Zug License: BSD Source: https://gitlab.com/andre-dietrich/rosshell Overview package nodes, enable run interact non-ros-programs. rosshell start program kind shell-command. kind interaction programs required, rosshellX receive send messages stdin, stdout, stderr. Usage required commands defined string: rosrun rosshell rosshell.py "command" rosrun rosshell rosshellX.py "command" examples included launch-files. Examples rosshell command generate random noise rosrun rosshell rosshell.py "aplay S16_LE /dev/urandom" Pipe results /dev/random text file rosrun rosshell rosshell.py "cat /dev/random test.txt" information current directory rosrun rosshell rosshell.py -Shal test.txt" rosshellX node require interaction stdin, stdout, stderr. rosshellX default subscribed /rosshellX/stdin publishes topic /rosshellX/stdout /rosshellX/stderr, type std_msgs/String. basic calculations simple commandline-calculator open shells start command: rosrun rosshell rosshellX.py print results stdout running: rostopic echo /rosshellX/stdout send inputs rostopic pub /rosshellX/stdin std_msgs/String rostopic pub /rosshellX/stdin std_msgs/String "sqrt(2.0)
" rostopic pub /rosshellX/stdin std_msgs/String rostopic pub /rosshellX/stdin std_msgs/String "quit
" interact bit stderr, simply subscribe rostopic echo /rosshellX/stderr publish nonsense rostopic pub /rosshellX/stdin std_msgs/String "wtf
" Additional functionality filesystem-information rosrun rosshell rosshellX.py -Shal" rosrun rosshell rosshellX.py _command:="ls -Shal" possibility change command topics, launch-file, rosshellX.launch <node pkg="rosshell" type="rosshellX.py" name="rosshellBC"> <param name="command" type="String" value="bc" <param name="stdout" type="String" value="bc/stdout" <param name="stdout" type="String" value="bc/stderr" <param name="stdin" type="String" value="bc/stdin" </node> Nodes rosshell run commands interaction possibilities. rosshellX run commands enables interaction stdin stdout. Subscribed Topics /rosshellX/stdin (std_msgs/String) standard topic stdin. Published Topics /rosshellX/stdout (std_msgs/String) current line stdout. /rosshellX/stderr (std_msgs/String) current line stderr. Parameters command (string, default: Commands executed. stdout (string, default: /rosshellX/stdout) Change topic stdout. stdin (string, default: /rosshellX/stdin) Change topic stdin. stderr (string, default: /rosshellX/stderr) Change topic stderr.
